test_cases.expected_result: deleteMany completes successfully with count=1 even when there are no related records to cascade-delete.

test_cases.title: Ticket status change OPEN->IN_PROGRESS triggers TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE automation rule
steps: Create an AutomationRule with triggerType='TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE', triggerConfig={fromStatus:'OPEN', toStatus:'IN_PROGRESS'}, actionType='SEND_NOTIFICATION', actionConfig={recipientId: userId, messageTemplate: 'הקריאה {ticketTitle} עברה לסטטוס {toStatus}'}
Create a ticket in OPEN status
Call updateTicket(ticketId, { status: 'IN_PROGRESS' })
Verify the Inngest 'ticket/status-change' event is sent with fromStatus='OPEN', toStatus='IN_PROGRESS'
In the fallback path, verify: prisma.automationRule.findMany queries rules with companyId, isActive=true, triggerType='TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE'
Verify the matching rule's triggerConfig.fromStatus matches 'OPEN' and triggerConfig.toStatus matches 'IN_PROGRESS'
Verify createNotificationForCompany is called with the formatted message replacing {ticketTitle} and {toStatus} with Hebrew label 'בטיפול'
test_cases.expected_result: The TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE automation fires. The notification message replaces {ticketTitle} with the ticket title and {toStatus} with 'בטיפול'. The rule is only triggered when fromStatus/toStatus match (or are 'any').

test_cases.title: SLA breach triggers SLA_BREACH automation rule and notification
steps: Create an AutomationRule with triggerType='SLA_BREACH', actionType='SEND_NOTIFICATION', actionConfig={recipientId: managerId}
Verify getServiceAutomationRules returns rules with triggerType IN ['TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE', 'SLA_BREACH']
When an SLA breach occurs (SlaBreach record created with status=PENDING), verify the SLA_BREACH automation is triggered
Verify the notification reaches the configured recipientId (manager)
test_cases.expected_result: SLA_BREACH automation rule fires when a breach is detected. The automation system queries rules with triggerType='SLA_BREACH' and executes SEND_NOTIFICATION action to alert the manager.

test_cases.title: SlaBreach cascade deletion when parent ticket is permanently deleted
steps: Create a ticket with CRITICAL priority
Trigger an SLA breach: create SlaBreach record with ticketId=ticket.id, breachType='RESOLVE', status='PENDING'
Verify the SlaBreach exists in the database
Call permanentlyDeleteTicket(ticket.id)
Verify SlaBreach records with ticketId=ticket.id are cascade-deleted (Prisma schema: SlaBreach -> Ticket onDelete: Cascade)
Query SlaBreach where ticketId=ticket.id and verify count=0
test_cases.expected_result: SlaBreach records are cascade-deleted when the parent ticket is deleted. The Prisma schema defines SlaBreach -> Ticket relation with onDelete: Cascade. No orphaned breach records remain.

test_cases.title: Ticket deletion cascades to TicketComment, TicketActivityLog, and SlaBreach
steps: Create a ticket and add 3 comments (TicketComment), trigger 2 activity logs (TicketActivityLog), and 1 SLA breach (SlaBreach)
Call permanentlyDeleteTicket(ticket.id)
Verify all 3 TicketComment records are deleted (onDelete: Cascade from schema)
Verify all TicketActivityLog records are deleted (onDelete: Cascade from schema)
Verify the SlaBreach record is deleted (onDelete: Cascade from schema)
Verify the ticket record itself is deleted (deleteMany count=1)
test_cases.expected_result: All child records (comments, activity logs, breaches) are cascade-deleted by PostgreSQL when the parent ticket is deleted. The deleteMany operation is atomic.

test_cases.title: Ticket.clientId set to null when client is soft-deleted (onDelete: SetNull)
steps: Create a client in the company
Create a ticket linked to that client (clientId=client.id)
Delete the client (soft delete with deletedAt timestamp, or hard delete if applicable)
Verify the ticket still exists with clientId set based on Prisma schema: Ticket -> Client onDelete: SetNull
Note: The schema uses onDelete: SetNull for Ticket -> Client relation
test_cases.expected_result: When a client is deleted, the Ticket.clientId field is set to null (per Prisma onDelete: SetNull). The ticket is not cascade-deleted; it remains intact without a client reference.

test_cases.title: Ticket detail view loads comments and activity logs with correct ordering and limits
steps: Create a ticket with 150 comments and 60 activity log entries
Call getTicketDetails(ticketId)
Verify comments are returned with orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } and take: 100 (first 100 most recent)
Verify activityLogs are returned with orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } and take: 50 (first 50 most recent)
Verify the select clause includes user: { select: { id, name } } for both comments and activity logs
test_cases.expected_result: getTicketDetails returns at most 100 comments and 50 activity logs, both sorted newest-first. This prevents excessive payload for tickets with long histories.

test_cases.title: getTicketStats returns aggregated counts with Redis caching (30s TTL)
steps: Create tickets in various statuses and a PENDING SLA breach
Call getTicketStats()
Verify it returns { open: N, inProgress: N, waiting: N, closed: N, breached: N }
Verify getCachedMetric is called with key 'service:stats:{companyId}' and TTL=30 seconds
Call getTicketStats() again within 30 seconds and verify Redis cache hit (no DB query)
Call invalidateServiceCache(companyId) and verify subsequent call hits DB
test_cases.expected_result: getTicketStats uses getCachedMetric with 30-second TTL. The stats include ticket counts grouped by status plus pending SLA breach count. Cache invalidation happens on ticket mutations.

test_cases.title: Admin can edit/delete comments by other users (ownership bypass for admin role)
steps: Authenticate as userA (role='basic'), add a comment to a ticket
Authenticate as userB (role='admin')
Call updateTicketComment(commentId, 'admin edited')
Verify: comment.userId !== userB.id BUT userB.role === 'admin', so the ownership check passes
Verify the comment content is updated to 'admin edited'
Call deleteTicketComment(commentId)
Verify the admin can delete the comment
test_cases.expected_result: Admin users bypass the comment ownership check (comment.userId !== user.id && user.role !== 'admin'). This is intentional behavior for moderation purposes.

test_cases.title: SLA recalculation on priority change via updateTicket
steps: Create SlaPolicy for MEDIUM (responseTimeMinutes=60, resolveTimeMinutes=480) and CRITICAL (responseTimeMinutes=15, resolveTimeMinutes=60)
Create a ticket with priority MEDIUM (slaDueDate ~ now+480min)
Call updateTicket(ticketId, { priority: 'CRITICAL' })
Verify the transaction detects data.priority !== current.priority
Verify it queries the CRITICAL SlaPolicy and recalculates SLA dates
Verify new slaDueDate ~ now + 60min (CRITICAL resolve time), not the old 480min
test_cases.expected_result: When priority changes, SLA deadlines are recalculated from the CRITICAL SlaPolicy. The updateTicket transaction detects the priority change and fetches the new SlaPolicy within the locked transaction.

test_cases.title: Empty title rejected on create and update
steps: Call createTicket({ title: '', status: 'OPEN', priority: 'MEDIUM', type: 'SERVICE' })
Verify validation: !data.title evaluates to true for empty string, throws new Error('Invalid title')
Create a valid ticket
Call updateTicket(ticketId, { title: '' })
Verify validation: data.title !== undefined && (!data.title || data.title.length > MAX_TITLE) throws Error('Invalid title')
test_cases.expected_result: Both createTicket and updateTicket reject empty string titles. The falsy check (!data.title) catches empty strings, null, and undefined.

test_cases.title: Large ticket list pagination does not exceed serviceRead rate limit
steps: Create 500 tickets in a single company
Page through all tickets using cursor-based pagination: getTickets() -> getTickets(cursor) -> ... (5 requests for 500 tickets at PAGE_SIZE=100)
Verify all 5 requests complete within the serviceRead rate limit of 60/user/min
Verify each request uses the @@index([companyId, status, updatedAt(sort: Desc)]) index from the Prisma schema
Measure response time per page: should be under 500ms for 100 tickets with included relations
test_cases.expected_result: 5 paginated requests stay well within the 60/min serviceRead rate limit. The cursor-based approach avoids OFFSET-based performance degradation. The composite index on [companyId, status, updatedAt DESC] supports the orderBy clause.

test_cases.title: Ticket status change fires Inngest event with fallback to direct automation execution
steps: Configure an AutomationRule: triggerType='TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE', triggerConfig={fromStatus:'any', toStatus:'RESOLVED'}, actionType='SEND_NOTIFICATION'
Update a ticket status to RESOLVED
Simulate Inngest.send failure (catch block in updateTicket)
Verify the fallback path executes: queries AutomationRule with companyId, isActive=true, triggerType='TICKET_STATUS_CHANGE'
Verify the fallback checks triggerConfig.fromStatus/toStatus against 'any' or exact match
Verify createNotificationForCompany is called directly in the fallback
test_cases.expected_result: When Inngest is unavailable, the direct fallback path queries and executes matching automation rules synchronously. The message template supports {ticketTitle}, {ticketId}, {fromStatus}, {toStatus} placeholders.

test_cases.title: SlaPolicy unique constraint prevents duplicate priority policies per company
steps: Create SlaPolicy for MEDIUM in companyId=1
Call updateSlaPolicy({ priority: 'MEDIUM', responseTimeMinutes: 30, resolveTimeMinutes: 120 })
Verify prisma.slaPolicy.upsert uses companyId_priority compound unique key
Verify the existing policy is UPDATED (not duplicated)
Query SlaPolicy where companyId=1 AND priority='MEDIUM' and verify exactly 1 record
test_cases.expected_result: The @@unique([companyId, priority]) constraint on SlaPolicy prevents duplicate priority policies per company. The upsert operation updates the existing policy rather than creating a duplicate.

test_cases.title: Create product with valid fields via createProduct (Serializable transaction with limit check)
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewServices permission flag (checked via hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewServices'))
Call createProduct({ name: 'Widget A', description: 'A widget', sku: 'WGT-001', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 49.99, cost: 25.00 })
Verify createProductSchema.parse validates: name min(1).max(200), price min(0).max(99999999.99), type enum('SERVICE','PRODUCT','PACKAGE')
Verify the Serializable transaction: tx.product.count < MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY (5000), then tx.product.create
Verify returned product has price=49.99 (Number), cost=25.00 (Number), isActive=true (default), type='PRODUCT'
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created within a Serializable isolation transaction that first counts existing products. Price is stored as Decimal(10,2) in DB but returned as Number(49.99). revalidatePath('/services') is called.

test_cases.title: Create product of type SERVICE with optional SKU
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'Consulting', type: 'SERVICE', price: 150.00 }) without sku or cost
Verify createProductSchema allows sku as optional (z.string().trim().max(100).optional())
Verify returned product has type='SERVICE', sku=undefined/null, cost=null
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created with type=SERVICE. SKU and cost are optional fields stored as null. The ProductType enum includes SERVICE, PRODUCT, PACKAGE.

test_cases.title: Update product details via updateProduct
steps: Create a product with price 49.99
Call updateProduct(productId, { name: 'Widget B', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 59.99 })
Verify updateProductSchema.parse validates the input
Verify prisma.product.update uses where: { id, companyId: user.companyId }
Verify returned product has name='Widget B', price=59.99 (Number)
test_cases.expected_result: Product name and price are updated. The update is scoped to companyId. Price is stored as Decimal(59.99) and returned as Number(59.99).

test_cases.title: Deactivate product by setting isActive=false via updateProduct
steps: Create an active product (isActive=true by default)
Call updateProduct(productId, { ...currentData, isActive: false })
Verify returned product has isActive=false
Call getProductsForDropdown()
Verify the deactivated product is NOT in the dropdown results (where: { isActive: true })
test_cases.expected_result: Product isActive is set to false. getProductsForDropdown excludes it because the query filters where: { companyId, isActive: true }.

test_cases.title: Create quote with auto-incremented quoteNumber via Serializable transaction
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewQuotes permission (checked via hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewQuotes'))
Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Test Client', items: [{ description: 'Item 1', quantity: 1, unitPrice: 100 }] })
Verify the Serializable transaction: findFirst quote with max quoteNumber, then nextQuoteNumber = (last?.quoteNumber ?? 0) + 1
Verify quote is created with status='DRAFT', shareToken=crypto.randomUUID(), quoteNumber=nextQuoteNumber
Verify Inngest event 'pdf/generate-quote' is fired after creation
Verify returned object has { id: quote.id }
test_cases.expected_result: Quote is created in DRAFT status with auto-incremented quoteNumber using Serializable isolation level. shareToken is a valid UUID from crypto.randomUUID(). PDF generation is triggered via Inngest.

test_cases.title: Update quote status from DRAFT to SENT via updateQuote
steps: Create a quote in DRAFT status
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...quoteData, status: 'SENT', items: [...] })
Verify updateQuoteSchema allows status: z.enum(['DRAFT','SENT','ACCEPTED','REJECTED']).optional()
Verify returned quote (after re-fetch) has status='SENT'
Verify Inngest event 'pdf/generate-quote' is fired with oldPdfUrl from previous version
test_cases.expected_result: Quote status changes from DRAFT to SENT. pdfUrl is reset to null on update. PDF regeneration is triggered via Inngest. The transaction: updates quote, deletes all items, recreates items.

test_cases.title: Update quote status from SENT to ACCEPTED
steps: Have a quote in SENT status
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...quoteData, status: 'ACCEPTED', items: [...] })
Verify the status field accepts 'ACCEPTED' via z.enum validation
Verify the quote status is now ACCEPTED
test_cases.expected_result: Quote status changes from SENT to ACCEPTED. The updateQuoteSchema permits this transition.

test_cases.title: Update quote status from SENT to REJECTED
steps: Have a quote in SENT status
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...quoteData, status: 'REJECTED', items: [...] })
Verify the quote status is now REJECTED
test_cases.expected_result: Quote status changes from SENT to REJECTED. The updateQuoteSchema permits this value.

test_cases.title: Add line items to quote linked to products via QuoteItem with productId FK
steps: Create a product with price 100.00
Create a quote with items: [{ productId: product.id, description: 'Widget', quantity: 5, unitPrice: 100.00 }]
Verify QuoteItem is created with productId FK linking to Product
Verify total is calculated as items.reduce((acc, item) => acc + item.quantity * item.unitPrice, 0) = 500.00
Call getQuoteById(quoteId) and verify items[0].product.name matches the product name
test_cases.expected_result: QuoteItem is linked to Product via productId FK. Total is calculated server-side as sum of quantity * unitPrice. getQuoteById includes product details via include relation.

test_cases.title: getProductsForDropdown returns only active products scoped to company
steps: Create 3 products in company: 2 with isActive=true, 1 with isActive=false
Call getProductsForDropdown()
Verify the query uses where: { companyId: user.companyId, isActive: true }
Verify exactly 2 products are returned
Verify each product has price as Number (not Decimal string)
test_cases.expected_result: Dropdown returns only active products. Query filters isActive=true. Products are mapped with price: Number(p.price) and cost: p.cost ? Number(p.cost) : null.

test_cases.title: getProductsForDropdown accessible without canViewServices (auth-only)
steps: Authenticate as a user WITHOUT canViewServices permission flag
Call getProductsForDropdown()
Verify the function only calls getCurrentUser() and does NOT check hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewServices')
Verify the endpoint returns products without permission error
test_cases.expected_result: getProductsForDropdown only requires authentication (getCurrentUser), not canViewServices permission. This allows quote editors to browse products without the service management permission.

test_cases.title: PDF generation triggered on quote creation via Inngest fire-and-forget
steps: Create a quote with line items
Verify inngest.send is called with: name='pdf/generate-quote', data={ quoteId, companyId }
Verify the Inngest call uses dedup ID: `pdf-quote-${companyId}-${quoteId}-${Math.floor(Date.now() / 5000)}`
Verify the send is fire-and-forget (.catch logs error but doesn't throw)
test_cases.expected_result: PDF generation Inngest event is fired after quote creation. The dedup ID prevents duplicate events within 5-second windows. Failure is logged but does not block the response.

test_cases.title: Create product with price at MAX_PRICE=99999999.99 boundary
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'Expensive', type: 'SERVICE', price: 99999999.99 })
Verify createProductSchema price validation: z.number().finite().min(0).max(99999999.99) passes
Verify the product is created with price stored as Decimal(99999999.99)
Verify getProducts returns price as Number(99999999.99)
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created at the Decimal(10,2) maximum boundary. Price field max(99_999_999.99) in validation matches the DB constraint.

test_cases.title: Create product with zero price
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'Free Item', type: 'SERVICE', price: 0.00 })
Verify createProductSchema price validation: min(0) allows 0.00
Verify the product is created with price=Decimal(0.00)
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created with zero price. The min(0) Zod validation allows 0.00 as a valid price.

test_cases.title: Create product with null cost (optional field)
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'No Cost', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 50.00 }) without specifying cost
Verify createProductSchema cost validation: z.number().optional() allows undefined
Verify the product is created with cost stored as null (Decimal? in schema)
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created with null cost. The Prisma schema defines cost as Decimal? (nullable).

test_cases.title: Create product at MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY=5000 boundary (exactly 5000th product)
steps: Set up a company with 4999 existing products
Call createProduct({ name: 'Product 5000', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 10 })
Verify Serializable transaction: count=4999, 4999 < 5000, so creation proceeds
Verify the 5000th product is created successfully
test_cases.expected_result: 5000th product is created because count (4999) < MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY (5000). The >= check only fails when count reaches 5000.

test_cases.title: Quote shareToken is a valid UUID format from crypto.randomUUID()
steps: Create a quote
Call getQuoteById to retrieve the shareToken
Validate shareToken matches UUID v4 format regex: /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-4[0-9a-f]{3}-[89ab][0-9a-f]{3}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/
Verify shareToken is unique (@@unique constraint in Prisma schema)
test_cases.expected_result: shareToken is a valid UUID v4 generated by crypto.randomUUID(). The Prisma schema has @unique constraint on Quote.shareToken.

test_cases.title: Quote creation requires at least 1 item (createQuoteSchema min(1))
steps: Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Test', items: [] })
Verify createQuoteSchema items validation: z.array(quoteItemSchema).min(1, 'At least one item is required')
Verify the request fails with error message containing 'At least one item is required'
test_cases.expected_result: createQuote throws an error because createQuoteSchema requires items.min(1). Empty items array is rejected on creation, though updateQuoteSchema allows min(0).

test_cases.title: Reject product creation exceeding MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY=5000 (ProductLimitError)
steps: Set up a company with exactly 5000 products
Call createProduct({ name: 'Product 5001', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 10 })
Verify Serializable transaction: count=5000, 5000 >= 5000, throws new ProductLimitError(5000)
Verify the error message is 'Maximum of 5000 products reached'
Verify ProductLimitError is re-thrown (not caught by sanitizeError)
test_cases.expected_result: ProductLimitError is thrown with message 'Maximum of 5000 products reached'. The Serializable isolation level ensures the count+create is atomic, preventing TOCTOU race.

test_cases.title: Reject product price exceeding MAX_PRICE=99999999.99
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'Too Expensive', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 100000000.00 })
Verify createProductSchema.parse throws Zod validation error: price max(99999999.99) exceeded
test_cases.expected_result: Zod schema validation fails before any database operation. The price constraint max(99_999_999.99) matches the Decimal(10,2) column maximum.

test_cases.title: Reject negative product price
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'Negative', type: 'PRODUCT', price: -10.00 })
Verify createProductSchema.parse throws Zod validation error: price min(0) violated
test_cases.expected_result: Zod schema validation fails. The price constraint min(0) rejects negative values.

test_cases.title: Reject product creation without required name (empty string)
steps: Call createProduct({ name: '', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 10 })
Verify createProductSchema.parse throws Zod validation error: name z.string().trim().min(1) fails on empty string
test_cases.expected_result: Zod schema validation fails. name field requires min(1) characters after trim().

test_cases.title: Product deletion fails with P2003 FK violation when referenced by QuoteItem
steps: Create a product
Create a quote with a QuoteItem referencing productId=product.id
Attempt to delete the product (if a deleteProduct function exists, or via direct Prisma call)
Verify Prisma throws error with code='P2003' (foreign key constraint violation)
Verify sanitizeError maps P2003 to: throw new Error('Cannot delete product: it is referenced by quote items. Deactivate it instead.')
test_cases.expected_result: Deletion fails with user-friendly message: 'Cannot delete product: it is referenced by quote items. Deactivate it instead.' The Prisma schema QuoteItem -> Product relation does not have onDelete: Cascade.

test_cases.title: Delete non-existent product returns P2025 mapped to 'Product not found.'
steps: Call updateProduct or delete with a non-existent product ID (e.g., id=999999)
Verify Prisma throws error with code='P2025' (record to update/delete does not exist)
Verify sanitizeError maps P2025 to: throw new Error('Product not found.')
test_cases.expected_result: sanitizeError maps Prisma P2025 code to 'Product not found.' message.

test_cases.title: Duplicate product SKU triggers P2002 mapped to 'Duplicate entry'
steps: Create a product with sku='SKU-001' in companyA
Attempt to create another product with sku='SKU-001' in the same company
Note: Product schema has @@index([companyId, name]) but NOT a unique constraint on [companyId, sku]
If the database has such a unique constraint, verify sanitizeError maps P2002 to: throw new Error('Duplicate entry')
test_cases.expected_result: If a unique constraint exists on [companyId, sku], creation fails with 'Duplicate entry'. Note: The current Prisma schema only has @@index([companyId, name]), not a unique index on SKU, so this may only trigger on actual DB-level constraints.

test_cases.title: Reject invalid quote status value not in QuoteStatus enum
steps: Create a quote in DRAFT status
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...data, status: 'APPROVED', items: [...] })
Verify updateQuoteSchema status: z.enum(['DRAFT','SENT','ACCEPTED','REJECTED']).optional() rejects 'APPROVED'
Verify the validation error is thrown before any database operation
test_cases.expected_result: Zod schema validation fails because 'APPROVED' is not in the enum ['DRAFT','SENT','ACCEPTED','REJECTED']. No state machine check exists; only enum validation.

test_cases.title: Quote status update does not enforce transition order (no state machine)
steps: Have a quote in ACCEPTED status
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...data, status: 'DRAFT', items: [...] })
Verify updateQuoteSchema allows any valid QuoteStatus value (DRAFT, SENT, ACCEPTED, REJECTED)
Note: The codebase does NOT implement a state machine for quote transitions
Verify the update succeeds because only Zod enum validation is applied
test_cases.expected_result: The update succeeds because the codebase does not enforce quote status transition order. Any valid QuoteStatus enum value is accepted via updateQuoteSchema. This is a known design decision (no state machine).

test_cases.title: Reject quote creation with clientId from different company (TOCTOU-safe inside transaction)
steps: Authenticate as user in companyA
Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Test', clientId: <clientId_in_companyB>, items: [...] })
Verify inside the Serializable transaction: tx.client.findFirst({ where: { id: clientId, companyId: user.companyId, deletedAt: null } })
Verify the client lookup returns null because clientId belongs to companyB, not companyA
Verify throws new Error('Invalid client') inside the transaction
test_cases.expected_result: Quote creation fails with Error('Invalid client'). The clientId validation is inside the Serializable transaction to prevent TOCTOU race conditions.

test_cases.title: Reject product CRUD without canViewServices permission (Forbidden error)
steps: Authenticate as a user without canViewServices permission flag
Call createProduct({ name: 'Test', type: 'PRODUCT', price: 10 })
Verify requireProductUser calls hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewServices') which returns false
Verify throws new Error('Forbidden')
test_cases.expected_result: Product creation throws Error('Forbidden') because canViewServices permission is required for all product mutations and reads (except getProductsForDropdown).

test_cases.title: Product access scoped to companyId via WHERE clause in all queries
steps: Create a product in companyA
Authenticate as a user in companyB with canViewServices permission
Call getProducts() - verify query includes where: { companyId: user.companyId } which is companyB
Verify the product from companyA is NOT in the returned list
Call updateProduct(productId_from_companyA, { ... }) - verify it fails with P2025 (record not found for that companyId)
test_cases.expected_result: Product data is isolated between companies. Every query filters by companyId=user.companyId. Updates/deletes use compound where: { id, companyId } so cross-company operations fail with P2025.

test_cases.title: Quote access scoped to companyId via WHERE clause
steps: Create a quote in companyA
Authenticate as a user in companyB with canViewQuotes permission
Call getQuotes() - verify query includes where: { companyId: user.companyId }
Verify the quote from companyA is NOT in the returned list
Call getQuoteById(quoteId_from_companyA) - verify query uses where: { id, companyId } and returns null
test_cases.expected_result: Quote data is isolated between companies. All queries filter by companyId=user.companyId.

test_cases.title: Public quote download via /api/p/quotes/[id]/download validates shareToken with tokensMatch
steps: Create a quote with a shareToken
GET /api/p/quotes/{quoteId}/download?token={valid_shareToken}
Verify CUID_RE regex (/^c[a-z0-9]{24,}$/) validates the quote ID format
Verify tokensMatch(token, quote.shareToken) returns true for valid token
Verify the PDF is served or 202 is returned if PDF is not ready
GET /api/p/quotes/{quoteId}/download?token=invalid_token
Verify tokensMatch returns false and response is 404 'Not found' (prevents quote existence enumeration)
test_cases.expected_result: Valid shareToken returns the quote PDF (200) or 202 if generating. Invalid token returns 404 (not 401/403) to prevent enumeration attacks. Rate limited at publicDownload: 20/IP/min.

test_cases.title: Product mutation rate limit 20 per user per minute (prefix 'prod-mut')
steps: Authenticate as a user with canViewServices permission
Send 20 createProduct/updateProduct requests within 1 minute
Verify each request checks checkActionRateLimit(String(user.id), RATE_LIMITS.productMutation)
Send the 21st mutation request
Verify checkActionRateLimit returns true (Redis key 'rl:prod-mut:{userId}' > max:20)
Verify throws new Error('Rate limit exceeded')
test_cases.expected_result: 21st product mutation is rejected. RATE_LIMITS.productMutation = { prefix: 'prod-mut', max: 20, windowSeconds: 60 }.

test_cases.title: Product read rate limit 60 per user per minute (prefix 'prod-read')
steps: Authenticate as a user with canViewServices permission
Send 60 getProducts or getProductsForDropdown requests within 1 minute
Send the 61st read request
Verify checkActionRateLimit(String(user.id), RATE_LIMITS.productRead) returns true
Verify throws new Error('Rate limit exceeded')
test_cases.expected_result: 61st product read is rejected. RATE_LIMITS.productRead = { prefix: 'prod-read', max: 60, windowSeconds: 60 }.

test_cases.title: Quote mutation rate limit 20 per user per minute (prefix 'qt-mut')
steps: Authenticate as a user with canViewQuotes permission
Send 20 createQuote/updateQuote/trashQuote requests within 1 minute
Send the 21st mutation request
Verify checkActionRateLimit returns true for RATE_LIMITS.quoteMutation (prefix 'qt-mut', max: 20, windowSeconds: 60)
Verify throws new Error('Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.')
test_cases.expected_result: 21st quote mutation is rejected with message 'Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.' (quotes use a more descriptive error message than products).

test_cases.title: Simultaneous quote creations get unique quoteNumbers via Serializable isolation
steps: Simultaneously send 5 createQuote requests for the same company
Each request uses Serializable transaction isolation: db.$transaction(async tx => { findFirst max quoteNumber; nextNumber = max + 1; create }, { isolationLevel: Serializable })
Verify all 5 quotes are created with unique, sequential quoteNumbers
Verify the @@unique([companyId, quoteNumber]) constraint is not violated
test_cases.expected_result: All 5 quotes receive unique quoteNumbers. Serializable isolation prevents dirty reads. Concurrent transactions may trigger P2034 serialization conflicts, which are retried up to MAX_RETRIES=2.

test_cases.title: Retry on P2034 serialization conflict during quote creation (MAX_RETRIES=2)
steps: Trigger a P2034 serialization conflict during createQuote (e.g., concurrent quoteNumber auto-increment)
Verify the retry loop catches err.code === 'P2034' and attempt < MAX_RETRIES (2)
Verify the loop continues to the next attempt
Verify the quote is eventually created successfully on retry
test_cases.expected_result: Quote creation retries on P2034 and succeeds within MAX_RETRIES=2 attempts. The for loop: for (let attempt = 0; ; attempt++) with break on success and continue on P2034.

test_cases.title: Product limit enforced atomically under concurrent creation with Serializable isolation
steps: Set up a company with 4999 products
Simultaneously send 3 createProduct requests
Each request uses Serializable transaction: count then create if count < 5000
Verify only 1 product is created (reaching 5000)
Verify the other 2 receive ProductLimitError('Maximum of 5000 products reached') or P2034 serialization conflicts
test_cases.expected_result: Serializable transaction isolation ensures count+create is atomic. Only 1 of the 3 concurrent requests succeeds. Others fail with ProductLimitError or are retried via withRetry but still fail after the first insert commits.

test_cases.title: withRetry handles transient DB errors (connection_failure 08006) on product queries
steps: Simulate a transient database error with code '08006' (connection_failure) during getProducts
Verify withRetry catches the error (TRANSIENT_CODES.has('08006') is true)
Verify it retries with exponential backoff: baseDelayMs=150, delay * 2^attempt * jitter
Verify the query succeeds on retry within maxRetries=2
test_cases.expected_result: withRetry transparently retries on connection failures. Product query succeeds on retry without exposing the transient error to the user.

test_cases.title: P2034 exhausts MAX_RETRIES=2 and returns generic error
steps: Trigger persistent P2034 serialization conflicts during createQuote on every attempt
Verify retry loop: attempt 0 (P2034, continue), attempt 1 (P2034, continue), attempt 2 (P2034, attempt >= MAX_RETRIES)
Verify the catch block logs 'createQuote transaction error' and throws new Error('Failed to create quote')
test_cases.expected_result: After exhausting MAX_RETRIES=2, the createQuote function logs the error and throws generic Error('Failed to create quote'). The P2034 internal error code is not exposed to the client.

test_cases.title: Decimal price converted to Number on read via explicit mapping
steps: Create a product with price Decimal(49.99) via createProduct
Call getProducts()
Verify the products.map transforms: price: Number(p.price), cost: p.cost ? Number(p.cost) : null
Verify the returned price is JavaScript Number 49.99, not a Prisma Decimal object or string
test_cases.expected_result: Price is stored as Decimal(10,2) in PostgreSQL but explicitly converted to JavaScript Number via Number(p.price) in getProducts and createProduct return values.

test_cases.title: Quote quoteNumber auto-increment is scoped per company via @@unique([companyId, quoteNumber])
steps: Create a quote in companyA - gets quoteNumber=1
Create a quote in companyB - gets quoteNumber=1 (independent sequence)
Create another quote in companyA - gets quoteNumber=2
Verify Prisma schema: @@unique([companyId, quoteNumber]) allows same quoteNumber across different companies
Verify each company's sequence is determined by: findFirst where companyId + quoteNumber not null, orderBy quoteNumber desc
test_cases.expected_result: CompanyA: quoteNumbers 1, 2. CompanyB: quoteNumber 1. Sequences are independent because the auto-increment logic queries MAX(quoteNumber) per company.

test_cases.title: Product with optional SKU left null
steps: Call createProduct({ name: 'No SKU Product', type: 'PACKAGE', price: 299.99 }) without sku
Verify createProductSchema allows sku: z.string().trim().max(100).optional()
Verify the product is created with sku=null in database (Product.sku is String? in schema)
Verify the product appears in getProducts and getProductsForDropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Product is created with null SKU. The field is optional in both the Zod schema and Prisma schema (String?).

test_cases.title: Product listing with MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY=5000 uses take limit
steps: Set up a company with 5000 products
Call getProducts()
Verify the query uses take: MAX_PRODUCTS_PER_COMPANY (5000) and orderBy: { name: 'asc' }
Verify the @@index([companyId, name]) index is used for efficient sorting
Measure response time: should handle 5000 products with the select clause (no unnecessary joins)
test_cases.expected_result: getProducts handles the maximum catalog size efficiently. The take: 5000 limit prevents unbounded queries. The select clause only fetches id, name, description, sku, type, price, cost, isActive.

test_cases.title: Full quote lifecycle DRAFT -> SENT -> ACCEPTED with PDF generation at each stage
steps: createQuote with items - verify status='DRAFT', Inngest 'pdf/generate-quote' fired
updateQuote with status='SENT' - verify status='SENT', pdfUrl reset to null, Inngest 'pdf/generate-quote' fired with oldPdfUrl
updateQuote with status='ACCEPTED' - verify status='ACCEPTED', Inngest 'pdf/generate-quote' fired again
test_cases.expected_result: Quote moves through DRAFT -> SENT -> ACCEPTED. PDF generation is triggered at every update (create and each updateQuote). pdfUrl is reset to null on update to force regeneration.

test_cases.title: Full quote lifecycle DRAFT -> SENT -> REJECTED
steps: createQuote - verify DRAFT status
updateQuote with status='SENT' - verify SENT
updateQuote with status='REJECTED' - verify REJECTED
test_cases.expected_result: Quote transitions through DRAFT -> SENT -> REJECTED. All transitions are valid QuoteStatus enum values.

test_cases.title: Quote operations require canViewQuotes permission (Forbidden error)
steps: Authenticate as a user without canViewQuotes permission flag
Call getQuotes()
Verify requireQuoteAccess calls hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewQuotes') which returns false
Verify throws new Error('Forbidden')
test_cases.expected_result: All quote operations (getQuotes, getQuoteById, createQuote, updateQuote, trashQuote, restoreQuote) throw Error('Forbidden') without canViewQuotes permission.

test_cases.title: TOCTOU protection: clientId deleted between validation and quote creation inside Serializable transaction
steps: Begin createQuote with a valid clientId
The Serializable transaction: tx.client.findFirst({ where: { id: clientId, companyId, deletedAt: null } })
Simulate the client being deleted (deletedAt set) concurrently
Verify the Serializable isolation level prevents the client from being visible as deleted within the transaction if deletion happens after the read
Or: if deletion happens before the read, verify findFirst returns null and throws Error('Invalid client')
test_cases.expected_result: TOCTOU protection: clientId validation is inside the Serializable transaction. If the client is deleted before the read, findFirst returns null and quote creation fails with Error('Invalid client'). Serializable isolation prevents phantom reads.

test_cases.title: Deactivating product referenced by QuoteItem does not break existing quotes
steps: Create a product (isActive=true) with id=P1
Create a quote with a QuoteItem referencing productId=P1
Call updateProduct(P1, { ...data, isActive: false })
Verify the product is deactivated (isActive=false)
Call getQuoteById(quoteId) and verify items[0].product.name still returns the product data
Verify QuoteItem.productId FK is NOT affected by isActive change (no cascade)
Verify getProductsForDropdown does NOT include the deactivated product for new quotes
test_cases.expected_result: Deactivating a product does not break existing QuoteItem references. The FK relationship is preserved. However, the product no longer appears in getProductsForDropdown (where isActive=true) for new quote creation.

test_cases.title: Quote soft-delete (isTrashed) preserves shareToken and allows restore
steps: Create a quote with shareToken and a cached pdfUrl
Call trashQuote(quoteId) - verify isTrashed=true, pdfUrl=null (cleared in transaction)
Verify the PDF file is deleted via UTApi.deleteFiles in background
Verify getQuotes(false) does NOT include the trashed quote (where isTrashed=false)
Verify getQuotes(true) includes the trashed quote (where isTrashed=true)
Call restoreQuote(quoteId) - verify isTrashed=false, Inngest 'pdf/generate-quote' is triggered
test_cases.expected_result: Quote soft-delete sets isTrashed=true and pdfUrl=null. Old PDF is cleaned up via UTApi. Restore sets isTrashed=false and triggers PDF regeneration. shareToken is preserved across trash/restore.

test_cases.title: Quote with 200 line items (max allowed by createQuoteSchema)
steps: Generate 200 quote items: each with description, quantity=1, unitPrice=10.00
Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Big Client', items: <200_items> })
Verify createQuoteSchema items: z.array(quoteItemSchema).min(1).max(200) passes for 200 items
Verify total is calculated as sum of 200 * (1 * 10.00) = 2000.00
Verify all 200 QuoteItem records are created in the transaction via tx.quote.create with items.create
Measure creation time: should complete within the 10s transaction timeout
test_cases.expected_result: Quote with 200 line items (the max allowed) is created successfully within the 10s transaction timeout. The total is calculated server-side as 2000.00.

test_cases.title: Reject quote with 201 line items exceeding max(200) validation
steps: Generate 201 quote items
Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Client', items: <201_items> })
Verify createQuoteSchema items: z.array(quoteItemSchema).max(200) rejects 201 items
test_cases.expected_result: Zod validation rejects the request before any database operation. The max(200) limit on items array prevents excessively large quotes.

test_cases.title: Quote ACCEPTED status contributes to QUOTES goal metric computation
steps: Create a Goal with metricType='QUOTES', targetType='SUM', status filter='ACCEPTED'
Create a quote with total=500.00 and update status to ACCEPTED
Call calculateMetricValue('QUOTES', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { status: 'ACCEPTED' })
Verify the QUOTES case in goal-computation.ts: prisma.quote.aggregate({ where: { companyId, isTrashed: false, status: 'ACCEPTED', createdAt: { gte, lte } }, _sum: { total: true } })
Verify the returned value includes the 500.00 from the accepted quote
test_cases.expected_result: The QUOTES metric in goal-computation correctly aggregates accepted quote totals. The where clause filters by status='ACCEPTED', isTrashed=false, and date range.

test_cases.title: Quote ACCEPTED count contributes to QUOTES goal metric (COUNT target type)
steps: Create a Goal with metricType='QUOTES', targetType='COUNT', status filter='ACCEPTED'
Create 3 quotes, accept 2 of them within the goal date range
Call calculateMetricValue('QUOTES', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { status: 'ACCEPTED' })
Verify prisma.quote.count returns 2
test_cases.expected_result: The QUOTES COUNT metric correctly counts the number of accepted quotes within the date range. Non-accepted quotes are excluded by the status filter.

test_cases.title: Public quote download returns 202 HTML page when PDF is not yet generated
steps: Create a quote without a cached pdfUrl (pdfUrl=null)
GET /api/p/quotes/{quoteId}/download?token={valid_shareToken}
Verify the response is HTTP 202 with Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8
Verify the HTML contains Hebrew text 'ה-PDF עדיין בהכנה' and auto-refresh meta tag: <meta http-equiv='refresh' content='5'>
Verify the response includes Retry-After: 5 header
Verify Inngest 'pdf/generate-quote' is triggered for recovery if quote was updated within 5 minutes
test_cases.expected_result: When PDF is not ready, the public download endpoint returns a 202 HTML page with auto-refresh every 5 seconds and a Hebrew message indicating the PDF is being prepared.

test_cases.title: Authenticated quote download falls back to inline PDF rendering when no cached PDF
steps: Create a quote without a cached pdfUrl
GET /api/quotes/{quoteId}/download (authenticated, with canViewQuotes permission)
Verify the endpoint detects !quote.pdfUrl
Verify it dynamically imports @react-pdf/renderer and QuotePdfTemplate
Verify registerFonts() is called before rendering
Verify the PDF is rendered inline and returned with Content-Type: application/pdf
Verify background Inngest job is triggered to cache the PDF for future downloads
test_cases.expected_result: Authenticated download falls back to server-side PDF rendering via @react-pdf/renderer when no cached PDF exists. The rendered PDF is returned immediately, and a background job caches it.

test_cases.title: Public download SSRF protection via isSafeStorageUrl validation
steps: Simulate a scenario where quote.pdfUrl contains a malicious internal URL (e.g., 'http://169.254.169.254/...')
GET /api/p/quotes/{quoteId}/download?token={valid_token}
Verify isSafeStorageUrl(quote.pdfUrl) returns false for non-whitelisted hosts
Verify the response is 500 with { status: 'error', message: 'PDF storage error' }
test_cases.expected_result: SSRF protection: isSafeStorageUrl validates that the pdfUrl host is in the allowed storage hosts list before making a server-side fetch. Malicious URLs return 500 error.

test_cases.title: Quote update preserves shareToken (not reset on update)
steps: Create a quote, note the shareToken
Call updateQuote(quoteId, { ...newData, items: [...] })
Verify the updateQuote transaction does NOT update shareToken (comment in code: 'shareToken is intentionally preserved')
Call getQuoteById(quoteId) and verify shareToken is unchanged
Verify the public download URL still works with the original shareToken
test_cases.expected_result: shareToken is intentionally preserved across updates. The updateQuote function only sets: clientId, clientName, clientEmail, clientPhone, clientTaxId, clientAddress, validUntil, status, title, total, isPriceWithVat, currency, exchangeRate, discountType, discountValue, pdfUrl=null. No shareToken reset.

test_cases.title: Quote items are fully replaced (delete all + recreate) on updateQuote
steps: Create a quote with 3 items (IDs: 1, 2, 3)
Call updateQuote with 2 new items (different descriptions)
Verify the transaction: tx.quoteItem.deleteMany({ where: { quoteId } }) removes all 3 original items
Verify tx.quoteItem.createMany creates 2 new items
Call getQuoteById and verify exactly 2 items exist with the new descriptions
test_cases.expected_result: updateQuote uses delete-all-then-recreate strategy for items. This ensures clean item management without tracking individual item additions/removals/reorders.

test_cases.title: trashQuote clears pdfUrl and triggers UploadThing cleanup
steps: Create a quote with cached pdfUrl='https://utfs.io/f/abc123.pdf'
Call trashQuote(quoteId)
Verify the transaction sets isTrashed=true AND pdfUrl=null atomically
Verify the background cleanup: import('uploadthing/server').then(UTApi).deleteFiles([fileKey])
Verify the file key is extracted from URL: url.pathname.split('/').pop()
test_cases.expected_result: trashQuote atomically sets isTrashed and clears pdfUrl in a single transaction. The old PDF file is cleaned up via UTApi in a fire-and-forget background task.

test_cases.title: Public download rate limit 20 per IP per minute (prefix 'pub-dl')
steps: Send 20 GET requests to /api/p/quotes/{quoteId}/download?token={valid_token} from the same IP within 1 minute
Send the 21st request from the same IP
Verify checkRateLimit(ip, RATE_LIMITS.publicDownload) returns a 429 NextResponse
Verify the 429 response body: { error: 'Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.' }
Verify the Retry-After header is set to '60' (windowSeconds)
test_cases.expected_result: Public download endpoint rate limits at 20 requests per IP per minute. RATE_LIMITS.publicDownload = { prefix: 'pub-dl', max: 20, windowSeconds: 60 }. Returns HTTP 429 with Retry-After header.

test_cases.title: QuoteItem cascade deletion when parent Quote is deleted
steps: Create a quote with 5 QuoteItems
Delete the quote (if a delete function exists, or simulate via Prisma)
Verify QuoteItem records are cascade-deleted (Prisma schema: QuoteItem -> Quote onDelete: Cascade)
Query QuoteItem where quoteId=quote.id and verify count=0
test_cases.expected_result: QuoteItem records are automatically cascade-deleted when the parent Quote is deleted. The Prisma schema defines onDelete: Cascade on the QuoteItem -> Quote relation.

test_cases.title: Quote discount validation: percent discount cannot exceed 100
steps: Call createQuote({ clientName: 'Test', items: [...], discountType: 'percent', discountValue: 101 })
Verify createQuoteSchema .refine checks: discountType === 'percent' && discountValue > 100
Verify the error message: 'Percent discount cannot exceed 100'
Call createQuote with discountType='percent', discountValue=100 - verify success
Call createQuote with discountType='fixed', discountValue=999999 - verify success (no 100% cap on fixed)
test_cases.expected_result: Percent discount is capped at 100 via Zod refine. Fixed discount has no such cap (only max 99999999.99 from Decimal constraint). The validation is applied on both create and update schemas.

test_cases.title: Quote item quantity validation: max 1,000,000
steps: Call createQuote with an item having quantity: 1000001
Verify quoteItemSchema: quantity z.number().int().positive().max(1_000_000) rejects 1000001
Call createQuote with an item having quantity: 1000000 - verify success
test_cases.expected_result: Item quantity is capped at 1,000,000 via Zod validation. Values above this are rejected before database operations.

test_cases.title: Create a retainer with MONTHLY frequency via createRetainer server action
steps: Authenticate as a user with canViewFinance permission
Call createRetainer({ title: 'SEO Retainer', clientId: <valid>, amount: 5000.00, frequency: 'monthly', startDate: '2026-02-01' })
Verify response has success=true and data contains id, title='SEO Retainer', amount=5000, frequency='monthly', status='active'
Verify the retainer is persisted with companyId matching user.companyId via prisma.retainer.findFirst({ where: { id: data.id, companyId: user.companyId } })
Verify nextDueDate is one month after startDate (2026-03-01) for default postpaid mode
test_cases.expected_result: Retainer is created with status='active', frequency='monthly', amount stored as Decimal(10,2), companyId matching user.companyId, and nextDueDate set to startDate + 1 month (postpaid default). revalidatePath is called for '/finance', '/finance/retainers', and '/'

test_cases.title: Update retainer amount and frequency via updateRetainer server action
steps: Create an active retainer with amount=3000.00 and frequency='monthly' via createRetainer
Call updateRetainer(retainerId, { amount: 4500.00, frequency: 'quarterly' })
Verify response has success=true and data.amount=4500 and data.frequency='quarterly'
Call getRetainerById(retainerId) and verify updatedAt timestamp is after the original createdAt
test_cases.expected_result: Retainer amount is updated to 4500.00 and frequency changed to 'quarterly'. updateRetainer uses an atomic prisma.$transaction to verify+update with companyId guard. Only whitelisted fields (title, amount, frequency, status, notes, nextDueDate) are accepted to prevent companyId/id injection

test_cases.title: Cancel an active retainer via updateRetainer status change
steps: Create an active retainer with frequency='monthly' via createRetainer
Call updateRetainer(retainerId, { status: 'cancelled' })
Call getRetainers() and verify the cancelled retainer still appears in results (deletedAt is still null)
Verify the retainer status field is 'cancelled'
test_cases.expected_result: Retainer status is set to 'cancelled' via updateRetainer. The retainer is NOT soft-deleted (deletedAt remains null), it simply has status='cancelled'. It still appears in getRetainers() results since that query filters by deletedAt=null, not by status

test_cases.title: View financial dashboard with aggregated revenue from multiple sources
steps: Create 2 active retainers (amount=2000 monthly, amount=6000 quarterly) via createRetainer
Create 3 paid transactions within the date range
Create 1 FinanceRecord with type='INCOME', status='COMPLETED' via addFinanceRecord
Call getFinanceRecords() to fetch dashboard data with stats
test_cases.expected_result: getFinanceRecords returns records array plus stats object containing { income, expenses, profit: income - expenses } aggregated via prisma.financeRecord.groupBy by type with _sum on amount. Dashboard aggregates revenue from FinanceRecord entries with companyId and deletedAt=null filters

test_cases.title: Revenue calculation sums paid transactions and one-time payments via calculateMetricValue
steps: Create Transaction records with status='paid' and paidDate within 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28 (amounts: 1000, 2500, 3500)
Create OneTimePayment records with status='paid' and paidDate within range (amounts: 500, 1500)
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 9000. The REVENUE case runs two parallel aggregate queries: prisma.transaction.aggregate({ where: { companyId, status: 'paid', OR: [paidDate in range, paidDate null + updatedAt in range] }, _sum: { amount: true } }) and prisma.oneTimePayment.aggregate with similar OR conditions including dueDate fallback. Result = transSum + paySum = 7000 + 2000 = 9000

test_cases.title: RETAINERS metric counts active retainers with COUNT targetType
steps: Create 5 retainers: 3 with status='active', 1 with status='paused', 1 with status='cancelled'
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 3. The RETAINERS case builds where clause { companyId, status: 'active' } and calls prisma.retainer.count({ where }), counting only active retainers regardless of date range

test_cases.title: RETAINERS metric sums amounts with SUM targetType
steps: Create 3 active retainers with amounts 1000.00, 2500.50, 3000.00
Create 1 paused retainer with amount 5000.00
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 6500.50. The RETAINERS case with targetType='SUM' calls prisma.retainer.aggregate({ where: { companyId, status: 'active' }, _sum: { amount: true } }), summing only the amount field of active retainers. Result = Number(result._sum.amount ?? 0) = 6500.50

test_cases.title: RETAINERS metric filters by frequency when specified
steps: Create 2 active retainers with frequency='monthly' (amounts 1000, 2000)
Create 1 active retainer with frequency='quarterly' (amount 3000)
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { frequency: 'monthly' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 3000. When filters.frequency is provided and not 'all', the where clause includes { frequency: 'monthly' }, so only the two monthly retainers are summed: 1000 + 2000 = 3000

test_cases.title: Revenue from FINANCE_RECORD source type sums INCOME records only
steps: Create 3 FinanceRecord entries with type='INCOME' and date within goal range (amounts: 500, 1200, 800) via addFinanceRecord
Create 2 FinanceRecord entries with type='EXPENSE' (amounts: 300, 400)
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { source: 'FINANCE_RECORD' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 2500. When filters.source='FINANCE_RECORD', the code sets type='INCOME' and queries prisma.financeRecord.aggregate({ where: { companyId, type: 'INCOME', date: { gte: startOfDay(startDate), lte: endOfDay(endDate) } }, _sum: { amount: true } }). EXPENSE records are excluded by the type='INCOME' filter

test_cases.title: Revenue from FINANCE_RECORD filtered by category via columnKey
steps: Create INCOME FinanceRecord with category='Marketing', amount=1000
Create INCOME FinanceRecord with category='Sales', amount=2000
Create INCOME FinanceRecord with category='Marketing', amount=500
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { source: 'FINANCE_RECORD', columnKey: 'Marketing' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 1500. When filters.columnKey is provided and not 'all', the where clause adds { category: filters.columnKey }, filtering to only INCOME records with category='Marketing': 1000 + 500 = 1500

test_cases.title: QUOTES metric counts accepted quotes with status filter
steps: Create 5 quotes: 2 with status='accepted', 2 with status='draft', 1 with status='rejected', all with isTrashed=false
Call calculateMetricValue('QUOTES', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { status: 'accepted' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 2. The QUOTES case builds where { companyId, isTrashed: false, createdAt: { gte, lte }, status: 'accepted' } and calls prisma.quote.count({ where }). Only quotes matching status='accepted' and isTrashed=false with createdAt in range are counted

test_cases.title: QUOTES metric sums total with SUM targetType
steps: Create 3 quotes with isTrashed=false and createdAt in range: total=5000, total=3000, total=2000
Create 1 trashed quote with total=10000 and isTrashed=true
Call calculateMetricValue('QUOTES', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 10000. With targetType='SUM', calls prisma.quote.aggregate({ where: { companyId, isTrashed: false, createdAt: { gte, lte } }, _sum: { total: true } }). Trashed quote excluded by isTrashed=false filter. Sum = 5000 + 3000 + 2000 = 10000

test_cases.title: CUSTOMERS metric counts clients created within date range
steps: Create 3 clients with createdAt within the goal date range (2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28)
Create 2 clients with createdAt outside the goal date range
Call calculateMetricValue('CUSTOMERS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 3. Without filters.tableId, the CUSTOMERS case queries prisma.client.count({ where: { companyId, createdAt: { gte: startOfDay(startDate), lte: endOfDay(endDate) } } })

test_cases.title: CUSTOMERS metric counts records from specific table when tableId filter provided
steps: Create a TableMeta with id=10 for the company
Create 5 records in tableId=10 with createdAt within the goal date range
Create 3 records in tableId=10 with createdAt outside the range
Call calculateMetricValue('CUSTOMERS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { tableId: 10 })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 5. When filters.tableId is provided, CUSTOMERS case queries prisma.record.count({ where: { companyId, tableId: 10, createdAt: { gte, lte } } }) instead of querying the Client table

test_cases.title: TASKS metric counts completed tasks by updatedAt date
steps: Create 4 tasks with status='completed_month' and updatedAt within goal range
Create 2 tasks with status='done' and updatedAt within the range
Create 3 tasks with status='todo'
Call calculateMetricValue('TASKS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 6. Default mode (not REDUCE) uses statusMap.COMPLETED = ['completed_month', 'done'] and filters by updatedAt in range. Query: prisma.task.count({ where: { companyId, status: { in: ['completed_month', 'done'] }, updatedAt: { gte, lte } } })

test_cases.title: TASKS metric in REDUCE mode counts remaining tasks without date filter
steps: Create 8 tasks with status='todo' for the company
Create 3 tasks with status='in_progress'
Call calculateMetricValue('TASKS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { taskGoalMode: 'REDUCE', status: 'TODO' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 8. In REDUCE mode, no date range filter is applied. statusMap['TODO'] = ['todo'], so query is prisma.task.count({ where: { companyId, status: { in: ['todo'] } } })

test_cases.title: CALENDAR metric counts events by startTime within date range
steps: Create 5 CalendarEvent records with startTime within goal date range
Create 2 CalendarEvent records with startTime outside the range
Call calculateMetricValue('CALENDAR', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 5. The CALENDAR case filters by { companyId, startTime: { gte: startOfDay(startDate), lte: endOfDay(endDate) } } via prisma.calendarEvent.count()

test_cases.title: CALENDAR metric filters by searchQuery on title and description
steps: Create CalendarEvent with title='Client Meeting' within date range
Create CalendarEvent with title='Team Standup' within date range
Create CalendarEvent with description='Meeting about project' and title='Planning' within date range
Call calculateMetricValue('CALENDAR', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { searchQuery: 'Meeting' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 2. When filters.searchQuery is provided, the code truncates to 200 chars via safeQuery = filters.searchQuery.slice(0, 200), then adds OR: [{ title: { contains: safeQuery, mode: 'insensitive' } }, { description: { contains: safeQuery, mode: 'insensitive' } }]. Matches 'Client Meeting' by title and 'Planning' by description containing 'Meeting'

test_cases.title: RECORDS metric counts records in specific table within date range
steps: Create a TableMeta with id=15 for the company
Create 7 records in tableId=15 with createdAt in the goal date range
Call calculateMetricValue('RECORDS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, { tableId: 15 })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 7. The RECORDS case with COUNT calls prisma.record.count({ where: { companyId, tableId: 15, createdAt: { gte, lte } } })

test_cases.title: RECORDS metric sums numeric column via raw SQL JSON extraction
steps: Create a TableMeta with id=20 and schemaJson containing column key='revenue'
Create 3 records in tableId=20 with data JSON: {revenue: '5000'}, {revenue: '3000'}, {revenue: '2000'}
Call calculateMetricValue('RECORDS', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { tableId: 20, columnKey: 'revenue' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 10000. Uses raw SQL: SELECT COALESCE(SUM(NULLIF(regexp_replace("data"->>'revenue', '[^0-9.\-]', '', 'g'), '')::numeric), 0)::text as total FROM "Record" WHERE companyId and tableId and createdAt range. The regexp_replace strips non-numeric chars before casting

test_cases.title: Revenue from retainer-specific transactions via TRANSACTIONS_RETAINER source
steps: Create Transaction records with status='paid', relatedType='retainer', paidDate in range (amounts: 2000, 3000)
Create Transaction records with status='paid', relatedType='one_time', paidDate in range (amount: 1000)
Create OneTimePayment records with status='paid' and notes containing 'Retainer' (amount: 1500)
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { source: 'TRANSACTIONS_RETAINER' })
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 6500. With source='TRANSACTIONS_RETAINER': transactionWhere adds relatedType='retainer' (filters to 2000+3000=5000), paymentWhere adds OR conditions requiring notes containing either Hebrew 'ריטיינר' or case-insensitive 'Retainer' (1500). Total = 5000 + 1500 = 6500

test_cases.title: Zero-amount retainer included in count but contributes 0 to sum
steps: Create an active retainer with amount=0.00 and frequency='monthly'
Create an active retainer with amount=1000.00 and frequency='monthly'
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: COUNT returns 2 (both retainers have status='active' so both are counted). SUM returns 1000.00 (zero-amount retainer contributes 0 to the aggregate sum via _sum.amount)

test_cases.title: Goal period with no matching data returns zero without error
steps: Create a date range (e.g., 2025-01-01 to 2025-01-31) where no transactions, payments, or finance records exist
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 0. Both prisma.transaction.aggregate and prisma.oneTimePayment.aggregate return { _sum: { amount: null } }, and Number(null ?? 0) = 0. No errors thrown when aggregating empty result sets

test_cases.title: RECORDS metric returns 0 immediately when no tableId provided
steps: Call calculateMetricValue('RECORDS', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 0 immediately without executing any database query. The RECORDS case checks `if (!filters.tableId) return 0;` as the first line, short-circuiting when tableId is missing

test_cases.title: Transaction with null paidDate falls back to updatedAt for date range matching
steps: Create a Transaction with status='paid', paidDate=null, updatedAt within the goal date range, amount=2000
Create a Transaction with status='paid', paidDate=null, updatedAt outside the range, amount=3000
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 2000. The transactionWhere uses OR: [{ paidDate: { gte, lte } }, { paidDate: null, updatedAt: { gte, lte } }]. The first transaction matches the second OR condition (paidDate=null AND updatedAt in range), the second does not

test_cases.title: OneTimePayment with null paidDate uses dueDate or updatedAt fallback
steps: Create OneTimePayment with status='paid', paidDate=null, dueDate within range, amount=1500
Create OneTimePayment with status='paid', paidDate=null, dueDate=null, updatedAt within range, amount=500
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 2000. The paymentWhere has three OR conditions: [{ paidDate in range }, { paidDate: null, dueDate in range }, { paidDate: null, updatedAt in range }]. First payment matches condition 2, second matches condition 3

test_cases.title: TASKS REDUCE mode with zero target uses referenceBase calculation
steps: Create 10 tasks with status='todo' for the company
Create a goal with metricType='TASKS', targetValue=0, filters={ taskGoalMode: 'REDUCE' }
Compute progress via enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: progressPercent = 0. In REDUCE mode with targetValue=0 and currentValue=10 (> targetValue), the code enters the `else` branch: progressPercent = Math.max(0, Math.round((targetValue / currentValue) * 100)) = Math.max(0, Math.round(0/10 * 100)) = 0

test_cases.title: Date range normalizes to startOfDay and endOfDay
steps: Create a REVENUE goal with startDate=2026-02-01T14:30:00Z and endDate=2026-02-28T08:00:00Z
Create a transaction with paidDate=2026-02-01T00:00:01Z (just after midnight on start day)
Create a transaction with paidDate=2026-02-28T23:59:59Z (just before midnight on end day)
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Both transactions are included. The code normalizes: const startDate = startOfDay(startDateRaw) (00:00:00.000) and const endDate = endOfDay(endDateRaw) (23:59:59.999). So even though the raw input was 14:30 and 08:00, the full day range is covered

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation on retainer queries in calculateMetricValue
steps: Create active retainers for companyId=1 (amounts: 1000, 2000)
Create active retainers for companyId=2 (amounts: 5000, 8000)
Call calculateMetricValue('RETAINERS', 'SUM', companyId=1, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 3000. The RETAINERS case always includes companyId in the where clause: { companyId, status: 'active' }. Company 2 retainers (13000 total) are completely invisible to company 1 queries

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation on revenue aggregation across Transaction and OneTimePayment
steps: Create paid transactions for companyId=1 totaling 5000
Create paid transactions for companyId=2 totaling 20000
Call calculateMetricValue('REVENUE', 'SUM', companyId=1, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 5000. Both transactionWhere and paymentWhere include { companyId } at the top level, ensuring all aggregations are scoped. Company 2's 20000 in transactions is excluded by the companyId filter

test_cases.title: SAFE_FIELD_NAME regex prevents SQL injection via columnKey in RECORDS SUM
steps: Call calculateMetricValue('RECORDS', 'SUM', companyId, startDate, endDate, { tableId: 1, columnKey: "'; DROP TABLE \"Record\";--" })
Verify the columnKey fails the /^[a-zA-Z0-9_\u0590-\u05FF]+$/ regex
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 0 immediately without executing any SQL query. The code checks `if (!SAFE_FIELD_NAME.test(filters.columnKey)) return 0;` before the raw SQL query, rejecting any columnKey containing quotes, semicolons, spaces, or other non-alphanumeric/Hebrew characters

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation across all eight metric types in calculateMetricValue
steps: Create data for companyId=1 and companyId=2 across: Client, Task, Quote, CalendarEvent, Record, Retainer, FinanceRecord, Transaction/OneTimePayment
For each metricType (REVENUE, SALES, CUSTOMERS, TASKS, RETAINERS, QUOTES, CALENDAR, RECORDS), call calculateMetricValue with companyId=1
test_cases.expected_result: Every metric type returns data scoped exclusively to companyId=1. Each case in the switch statement includes companyId in its prisma query where clause. No cross-company data leakage occurs in any metric calculation

test_cases.title: Concurrent retainer status updates handled by prisma.$transaction with TOCTOU prevention
steps: Create an active retainer
Simultaneously call updateRetainer(id, { status: 'paused' }) and updateRetainer(id, { status: 'cancelled' })
Both calls use prisma.$transaction with findFirst+update pattern
test_cases.expected_result: Both updates use atomic prisma.$transaction({ maxWait: 5000, timeout: 10000 }) wrapping findFirst+update. One update succeeds first, the second either succeeds with last-write-wins or retries via withRetry on transient errors. No data corruption occurs because each transaction verifies ownership before updating

test_cases.title: Inngest fire-and-forget triggers dashboard cache refresh after goal mutation
steps: Create a new goal via createGoal server action
Verify that inngest.send is called with event name 'dashboard/refresh-goals' and data: { companyId: user.companyId }
test_cases.expected_result: Inngest event is dispatched with dedup ID: `goals-refresh-${companyId}-${Math.floor(Date.now() / 5000)}` (5-second window). The .catch() handler ensures the mutation succeeds even if Inngest fails. This is fire-and-forget so it doesn't block the response

test_cases.title: Soft delete preserves retainer data via deleteRetainer with deletedAt timestamp
steps: Create an active retainer via createRetainer
Call deleteRetainer(retainerId)
Directly query prisma.retainer.findFirst({ where: { id: retainerId } }) without deletedAt=null filter
test_cases.expected_result: Retainer record still exists in the database with deletedAt set to a non-null timestamp. All original fields (amount, frequency, client relation) are preserved for audit trail. The deleteRetainer function also soft-deletes associated retainer payments via tx.oneTimePayment.updateMany with notes containing `ריטיינר #${id}`

test_cases.title: Soft-deleted finance records excluded from getFinanceRecords active queries
steps: Create 3 INCOME FinanceRecords via addFinanceRecord
Soft-delete 1 record via deleteFinanceRecord(id) which sets deletedAt=new Date()
Call getFinanceRecords()
test_cases.expected_result: Only 2 non-deleted finance records are returned. getFinanceRecords always includes `deletedAt: null` in the where clause. The soft-deleted record is hidden but preserved in the database. Stats (income, expenses, profit) are also computed with the deletedAt=null filter

test_cases.title: SALES metric counts transactions and payments combined
steps: Create 3 Transaction records with status='paid' within date range
Create 2 OneTimePayment records with status='paid' within date range
Call calculateMetricValue('SALES', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 5. The SALES case (falls through from REVENUE) with targetType='COUNT' calls both prisma.transaction.count and prisma.oneTimePayment.count in parallel via Promise.all, then returns transCount + payCount = 3 + 2 = 5

test_cases.title: Unknown metricType returns zero from default switch case
steps: Call calculateMetricValue('UNKNOWN_TYPE', 'COUNT', companyId, startDate, endDate, {})
test_cases.expected_result: Returns 0. The switch statement has a `default: return 0;` case that handles any metricType not matching REVENUE, SALES, CUSTOMERS, TASKS, RETAINERS, QUOTES, CALENDAR, or RECORDS. No error is thrown

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects non-positive amount with 'Amount must be positive'
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewFinance permission
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 0, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: -500, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: NaN, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: Infinity, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
test_cases.expected_result: All four calls throw Error('Amount must be positive'). The validation checks: typeof data.amount !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(data.amount) || data.amount <= 0. Zero, negative, NaN, and Infinity all fail this check

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects missing or invalid title
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: '', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'x'.repeat(201), amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: null, amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date() })
test_cases.expected_result: All calls throw Error('Title is required and must be under 200 characters'). The validation checks: !data.title || typeof data.title !== 'string' || data.title.length > 200

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects invalid type (not INCOME or EXPENSE)
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'CREDIT', date: new Date() })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: '', date: new Date() })
test_cases.expected_result: Both calls throw Error('Type must be INCOME or EXPENSE'). The validation checks !['INCOME', 'EXPENSE'].includes(data.type)

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects invalid status values
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), status: 'APPROVED' })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), status: 'active' })
test_cases.expected_result: Both calls throw Error('Invalid status'). The validation checks: data.status && !['PENDING', 'COMPLETED', 'CANCELLED'].includes(data.status). Only the three FinanceRecordStatus enum values are accepted

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects invalid date
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: 'not-a-date' })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date('invalid') })
test_cases.expected_result: Both calls throw Error('Invalid date'). The validation checks: !(data.date instanceof Date) || isNaN(data.date.getTime()). The string 'not-a-date' fails instanceof Date, and new Date('invalid') produces NaN for getTime()

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects invalid clientId
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), clientId: -1 })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), clientId: 0 })
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), clientId: 1.5 })
test_cases.expected_result: All calls throw Error('Invalid client ID'). The validation checks: data.clientId !== undefined && (!Number.isInteger(data.clientId) || data.clientId <= 0). Negative, zero, and non-integer values are all rejected

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects clientId belonging to a different company
steps: Create a client belonging to companyId=2
Authenticate as user in companyId=1
Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), clientId: <company2ClientId> })
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('Invalid client'). Inside the prisma.$transaction, tx.client.findFirst({ where: { id: data.clientId, companyId: user.companyId, deletedAt: null } }) returns null because the client belongs to a different company, triggering the 'Invalid client' error

test_cases.title: createRetainer rejects invalid frequency values
steps: Call createRetainer({ title: 'Test', clientId: <valid>, amount: 1000, frequency: 'weekly', startDate: '2026-02-01' })
Call createRetainer({ title: 'Test', clientId: <valid>, amount: 1000, frequency: 'yearly', startDate: '2026-02-01' })
test_cases.expected_result: Both return { success: false, error: 'Invalid frequency' }. The validation checks !['monthly', 'quarterly', 'annually'].includes(frequency). Note: 'yearly' is NOT valid -- only 'annually' is accepted per RetainerFrequency enum

test_cases.title: createRetainer rejects non-positive amount
steps: Call createRetainer({ title: 'Test', clientId: <valid>, amount: 0, frequency: 'monthly', startDate: '2026-02-01' })
Call createRetainer({ title: 'Test', clientId: <valid>, amount: -100, frequency: 'monthly', startDate: '2026-02-01' })
test_cases.expected_result: Both return { success: false, error: 'Amount must be a positive number' }. Validation: typeof amount !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(amount) || amount <= 0

test_cases.title: updateRetainer rejects invalid retainer status
steps: Create a valid active retainer
Call updateRetainer(id, { status: 'inactive' })
Call updateRetainer(id, { status: 'ACTIVE' })
test_cases.expected_result: Both return { success: false, error: 'Invalid retainer status: inactive' } and { success: false, error: 'Invalid retainer status: ACTIVE' } respectively. Validation: !['active', 'paused', 'cancelled'].includes(data.status). Note: status values are lowercase per RetainerStatus enum

test_cases.title: createSyncRule rejects TABLE source without sourceId
steps: Call createSyncRule({ name: 'Test', targetType: 'INCOME', sourceType: 'TABLE', fieldMapping: { amountField: 'amount', dateField: 'date', titleField: 'title' } })
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('sourceId is required for TABLE source type'). The code explicitly checks: if (data.sourceType === 'TABLE' && !data.sourceId) throw new Error('sourceId is required for TABLE source type')

test_cases.title: createSyncRule rejects TABLE source pointing to deleted or non-existent table
steps: Authenticate as user in companyId=1
Call createSyncRule({ name: 'Sync', targetType: 'INCOME', sourceType: 'TABLE', sourceId: 99999, fieldMapping: { amountField: 'amount', dateField: 'date', titleField: 'title' } })
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('Invalid sourceId -- table not found'). The code verifies table ownership: prisma.tableMeta.findFirst({ where: { id: data.sourceId, companyId: user.companyId } }) returns null for non-existent or cross-company tables

test_cases.title: Duplicate sync rule creation rejected by unique constraint
steps: Create a sync rule with sourceType='TABLE' and sourceId=5 for companyId=1
Attempt to create another sync rule with the same sourceType='TABLE' and sourceId=5 for companyId=1
test_cases.expected_result: Second creation throws Prisma unique constraint error. The FinanceSyncRule model has @@unique([companyId, sourceType, sourceId]) which prevents duplicate sync rules for the same company+source combination

test_cases.title: deleteFinanceRecord rejects invalid (non-integer, <= 0) record IDs
steps: Call deleteFinanceRecord(0)
Call deleteFinanceRecord(-5)
Call deleteFinanceRecord(1.5)
test_cases.expected_result: All return { success: false, error: 'Invalid record ID' }. The validation: !Number.isInteger(id) || id <= 0 catches zero, negative, and non-integer values before any database query

test_cases.title: deleteFinanceRecord returns not found for record belonging to different company
steps: Create a FinanceRecord belonging to companyId=2
Authenticate as user in companyId=1
Call deleteFinanceRecord(recordId)
test_cases.expected_result: Returns { success: false, error: 'Record not found' }. Inside the transaction, tx.financeRecord.findFirst({ where: { id, companyId: user.companyId, deletedAt: null } }) returns null because the record belongs to company 2, not company 1

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rate limited at 30 mutations per minute
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewFinance permission
Call addFinanceRecord with valid data 31 times within 60 seconds
test_cases.expected_result: The 31st call throws Error('Rate limit exceeded'). The server action imports RATE_LIMITS.financeMutation ({ prefix: 'fin-mut', max: 30, windowSeconds: 60 }) and calls checkActionRateLimit before processing
