test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects description exceeding 5000 characters
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), description: 'x'.repeat(5001) })
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('Description must be under 5000 characters'). Validation: data.description && (typeof data.description !== 'string' || data.description.length > 5000)

test_cases.title: addFinanceRecord rejects category exceeding 200 characters
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Test', amount: 100, type: 'INCOME', date: new Date(), category: 'x'.repeat(201) })
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('Category must be under 200 characters'). Validation: data.category && (typeof data.category !== 'string' || data.category.length > 200)

test_cases.title: Finance sync trigger fires when payment status updated to paid
steps: Create a OneTimePayment with status='pending' via createPayment
Call updatePayment(id, { status: 'paid' })
Verify triggerSyncByType is called with (companyId, 'PAYMENTS_RETAINERS')
test_cases.expected_result: After the transaction commits, updatePayment checks if the new status is in PAID_STATUS_VARIANTS. If so, it calls triggerSyncByType(clientCompanyId, 'PAYMENTS_RETAINERS') which enqueues a finance sync job via Inngest. The sync is intentionally outside the transaction to avoid holding locks during Inngest enqueue

test_cases.title: markRetainerAsPaid creates OneTimePayment records and triggers sync
steps: Create an active retainer with amount=5000 and frequency='monthly'
Call markRetainerAsPaid(retainerId, 3) to generate 3 months of payments
Verify 3 OneTimePayment records are created with status='paid' and sequential dates
Verify triggerSyncByType is called with (companyId, 'PAYMENTS_RETAINERS')
test_cases.expected_result: Three payment records are batch-created via tx.oneTimePayment.createMany with amount=5000 each, notes containing `ריטיינר #${retainerId}`, and dates advancing monthly. The retainer's nextDueDate is updated to 3 months after the original. After the RepeatableRead transaction commits, triggerSyncByType fires for PAYMENTS_RETAINERS sync

test_cases.title: deleteSyncRule cascades to delete all linked finance records atomically
steps: Create a FinanceSyncRule with sourceType='TABLE' and sourceId=10
Run the sync to create 20 FinanceRecord entries with syncRuleId set
Call deleteSyncRule(ruleId)
Query FinanceRecord entries with syncRuleId=ruleId
test_cases.expected_result: Zero FinanceRecords remain for the deleted rule. deleteSyncRule wraps both operations in prisma.$transaction: first tx.financeRecord.deleteMany({ where: { syncRuleId: id, companyId } }), then tx.financeSyncRule.delete({ where: { id, companyId } }). If either fails, the entire transaction rolls back preventing orphaned records

test_cases.title: deleteFinanceRecord cascades source record deletion for TABLE sync rules
steps: Create a FinanceSyncRule with sourceType='TABLE' and sourceId=<tableId>
Create a Record in the table and sync it to create a FinanceRecord with originId=recordId
Call deleteFinanceRecord(financeRecordId)
Query for the original Record by ID
test_cases.expected_result: Both the FinanceRecord (soft-deleted with deletedAt set) and the source Record (hard-deleted via deleteRecordWithCleanup) are removed. The cascade only fires when financeRecord.syncRule.sourceType === 'TABLE' AND financeRecord.originId is set. deleteRecordWithCleanup is called with skipFinanceCascade=true to prevent infinite loops

test_cases.title: getFinanceRecords pagination limits response to 500 records maximum
steps: Create 600 FinanceRecord entries for the company
Call getFinanceRecords({ take: 1000 })
Verify response contains at most 500 records
test_cases.expected_result: Returns exactly 500 records with hasMore=true and a nextCursor value. The take parameter is clamped: const take = Math.min(filters?.take ?? 500, 500). The query fetches take+1 rows to determine hasMore, then slices to take

test_cases.title: Finance records and stats computed in parallel via Promise.all
steps: Create 200 FinanceRecord entries with mixed types (INCOME/EXPENSE)
Call getFinanceRecords() and measure that records list and groupBy stats run concurrently
test_cases.expected_result: The function executes prisma.financeRecord.findMany and prisma.financeRecord.groupBy in parallel via Promise.all([...]), reducing latency compared to sequential execution. Both queries share the same where clause including companyId and deletedAt=null

test_cases.title: Finance record creation defaults status to COMPLETED when not specified
steps: Call addFinanceRecord({ title: 'Ad Spend', amount: 500, type: 'EXPENSE', date: new Date() }) without specifying status
Verify the created record has status='COMPLETED'
test_cases.expected_result: When data.status is not provided, the create uses (data.status || 'COMPLETED') as the default, matching the Prisma model default of FinanceRecordStatus.COMPLETED. This provides a sensible UX default where most manual entries are already completed

test_cases.title: Retainer prepaid mode sets nextDueDate to startDate (immediate billing)
steps: Call createRetainer({ title: 'Prepaid', clientId: <valid>, amount: 2000, frequency: 'monthly', startDate: '2026-03-01', paymentMode: 'prepaid' })
Verify nextDueDate equals startDate (2026-03-01)
test_cases.expected_result: With paymentMode='prepaid', the nextDueDate is NOT advanced by one interval. The code skips the switch block when paymentMode === 'prepaid', so nextDueDate = startDate = 2026-03-01, meaning the first payment is due immediately

test_cases.title: Fixed expenses processing removed from read path (Issue 27)
steps: Call getFinanceRecords() as an authenticated user
Verify that processFixedExpenses() is NOT called during the read operation
test_cases.expected_result: The getFinanceRecords function has a comment '// Issue 27: Removed processFixedExpenses() from read path -- use scheduled cron instead'. This confirms that the expensive side effect was removed from the read path to prevent performance degradation and moved to a scheduled cron job

test_cases.title: enqueueSyncJob prevents duplicate running jobs via Serializable transaction
steps: Create a sync rule and call enqueueSyncJob(ruleId) to create a QUEUED job
Before the job completes, call enqueueSyncJob(ruleId) again
test_cases.expected_result: Second call returns { jobId: existingJob.id } with isNew=false. The Serializable transaction checks for an existing job with status in ['QUEUED', 'RUNNING']. If found, it returns the existing job ID without creating a new one or sending a new Inngest event. This prevents duplicate sync processing

test_cases.title: Create a financial goal via POST /api/finance/goals with valid data
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewGoals and canViewFinance permissions

POST to /api/finance/goals with body: { name: 'Monthly Revenue Target', metricType: 'REVENUE', targetType: 'SUM', targetValue: 50000, periodType: 'MONTHLY', startDate: '2026-02-01T00:00:00.000Z', endDate: '2026-02-28T00:00:00.000Z', warningThreshold: 70, criticalThreshold: 50 }

Verify response status is 200 and body contains id, name, metricType, targetType, targetValue, periodType, startDate, endDate, warningThreshold, criticalThreshold, isActive, isArchived, order, notes, createdAt, updatedAt
test_cases.expected_result: Goal is created via Serializable transaction (prisma.$transaction with isolationLevel: Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel.Serializable) that atomically counts active goals and creates if under MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY (50). Response includes all selected fields. targetValue stored as Decimal(15,2). Inngest 'dashboard/refresh-goals' event dispatched and goals cache invalidated

test_cases.title: Update a financial goal via PATCH /api/finance/goals/{id}
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=10000 and name='Q1 Sales'
PATCH /api/finance/goals/{id} with body: { name: 'Q1 Sales Updated', targetValue: 15000 }
Verify the goal is updated and Inngest dashboard refresh is triggered
test_cases.expected_result: Goal name and targetValue are updated, existing fields not in the payload remain unchanged. Inngest event 'dashboard/refresh-goals' is dispatched with dedup ID based on companyId and 5-second time window

test_cases.title: Delete a goal sets isArchived=true and isActive=false
steps: Create an active goal
DELETE /api/finance/goals/{id}
Query goal directly via prisma.goal.findUnique({ where: { id } })
test_cases.expected_result: Goal is not permanently removed. The DELETE handler sets isArchived=true and isActive=false via prisma.goal.update. Inngest cache refresh is triggered. Goal no longer appears in GET /api/finance/goals (which filters isArchived=false)

test_cases.title: Archive a goal with progress snapshot via toggleGoalArchive
steps: Create a goal with metricType='REVENUE', targetValue=10000
Generate revenue data so currentValue reaches 7500 (75% progress)
Call toggleGoalArchive(goalId, true)
Directly read the goal's filters JSON from database
test_cases.expected_result: Goal is archived. The function first fetches the goal, calls enrichGoalsWithProgress to compute live values, then wraps the archive in a prisma.$transaction that reads the fresh goal (preventing stale data) and updates filters with _archivedSnapshot containing: { currentValue: 7500, progressPercent: 75, status: 'ON_TRACK', projectedValue: <computed>, daysRemaining: <computed>, recommendation: <string>, archivedAt: <ISO timestamp> }

test_cases.title: Restore an archived goal via toggleGoalArchive(id, false)
steps: Archive a goal using toggleGoalArchive(goalId, true)
Restore it using toggleGoalArchive(goalId, false)
Call getGoalsWithProgress() and verify the restored goal appears
test_cases.expected_result: Goal isArchived is set back to false via prisma.goal.update({ where: { id, companyId }, data: { isArchived: false } }). Goal reappears in active goals list. Inngest 'dashboard/refresh-goals' event dispatched. revalidatePath called for both '/finance/goals' and '/finance/goals/archive'

test_cases.title: enrichGoalsWithProgress computes currentValue, progressPercent, status, and projectedValue
steps: Create a CUSTOMERS goal with targetValue=100, startDate 30 days ago, endDate 30 days from now
Create 60 clients within the date range
Call getGoalsWithProgress() which delegates to enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: Goal returns: currentValue=60, progressPercent=60 (Math.round(60/100 * 100)), status='WARNING' (60 < warningThreshold=70 but >= criticalThreshold=50), daysRemaining=30, dailyRate=60/30=2/day, projectedValue=Math.round(2 * 60)=120. recommendation shows required daily pace

test_cases.title: Goal created without thresholds uses defaults of 70 and 50
steps: POST to /api/finance/goals without warningThreshold or criticalThreshold fields
Fetch the created goal
test_cases.expected_result: Goal is created with warningThreshold=70 and criticalThreshold=50 as defined in the Zod schema defaults: warningThreshold: z.number().int().min(0).max(100).default(70) and criticalThreshold: z.number().int().min(0).max(100).default(50)

test_cases.title: Reorder goals via updateGoalOrder using raw SQL with unnest+generate_series
steps: Create 5 goals for the company with IDs [10, 20, 30, 40, 50]
Call updateGoalOrder([50, 30, 10, 40, 20])
Fetch goals ordered by order field
test_cases.expected_result: Goals are reordered using a single raw SQL: UPDATE "Goal" AS g SET "order" = v.new_order, "updatedAt" = NOW() FROM (SELECT unnest(goalIds::int[]) AS id, generate_series(0, length-1) AS new_order) AS v WHERE g.id = v.id AND g.companyId = user.companyId. Goal 50 gets order=0, 30 gets order=1, etc.

test_cases.title: Archived goals with snapshot serve from stored data without metric queries
steps: Archive a goal that has an _archivedSnapshot in its filters
Call getArchivedGoals()
Verify the goal data comes from the stored snapshot
test_cases.expected_result: Archived goals with _archivedSnapshot are reconstructed entirely from stored data: currentValue, progressPercent, status, projectedValue, daysRemaining, recommendation. ZERO calculateMetricValue calls are made for these goals. The code iterates goals and separates them into goalsWithSnapshot and goalsWithoutSnapshot arrays

test_cases.title: Legacy archived goals without snapshot are live-computed and backfilled
steps: Directly set a goal to isArchived=true without creating an _archivedSnapshot (simulating pre-snapshot legacy data)
Call getArchivedGoals()
Query the goal's filters JSON to verify snapshot was persisted
test_cases.expected_result: Legacy goals are live-computed via enrichGoalsWithProgress(goalsWithoutSnapshot, companyId). The snapshot is then persisted asynchronously using Promise.allSettled(snapshotPromises) so failures don't block the response. Each snapshot includes currentValue, progressPercent, status, projectedValue, daysRemaining, recommendation, archivedAt

test_cases.title: getGoalCreationData returns clients and formatted tables with parsed columns
steps: Create 3 clients and 2 TableMeta records with schemaJson in different formats: one as Array, one as {columns: [...]}
Call getGoalCreationData()
test_cases.expected_result: Returns { clients, tables }. Clients: max 500, sorted by name asc via prisma.client.findMany({ orderBy: { name: 'asc' }, take: 500 }). Tables: max 100. Columns parsed from schemaJson supporting Array format, {columns:[]} format, and {fields:[]} format. Each column mapped to { id: c.id||c.name, key: c.key||c.name||c.id, name: c.label||c.displayName||c.name, type: c.type }

test_cases.title: Goal status ON_TRACK when progressPercent >= warningThreshold
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=100, warningThreshold=70, criticalThreshold=50
Generate data so currentValue=75 (progressPercent=75)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: status = 'ON_TRACK'. The status logic: if (progressPercent >= 100) status='EXCEEDED'; else if (progressPercent >= goal.warningThreshold) status='ON_TRACK'; else if (progressPercent >= goal.criticalThreshold) status='WARNING'; else status='CRITICAL'. 75 >= 70 is true so ON_TRACK

test_cases.title: Goal status WARNING when between critical and warning thresholds
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=100, warningThreshold=70, criticalThreshold=50
Generate data so currentValue=55 (progressPercent=55)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: status = 'WARNING'. 55 < 100 (not EXCEEDED), 55 < 70 (not ON_TRACK), 55 >= 50 (is WARNING). The status is WARNING because progressPercent (55) >= criticalThreshold (50) but < warningThreshold (70)

test_cases.title: Goal status CRITICAL when progressPercent < criticalThreshold
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=100, warningThreshold=70, criticalThreshold=50
Generate data so currentValue=30 (progressPercent=30)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: status = 'CRITICAL'. 30 < 100 (not EXCEEDED), 30 < 70 (not ON_TRACK), 30 < 50 (not WARNING), so falls through to CRITICAL

test_cases.title: Goal status EXCEEDED when progressPercent >= 100
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=100, warningThreshold=70, criticalThreshold=50
Generate data so currentValue=120 (progressPercent=120)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: status = 'EXCEEDED'. The EXCEEDED check is first: if (progressPercent >= 100) status = 'EXCEEDED'. progressPercent = Math.round(120/100 * 100) = 120, which is >= 100

test_cases.title: Custom thresholds (90/80) override defaults and affect status computation
steps: Create a goal with warningThreshold=90 and criticalThreshold=80
Generate data so currentValue=85% of target
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: status = 'WARNING'. With custom thresholds: 85 < 100 (not EXCEEDED), 85 < 90 (not ON_TRACK), 85 >= 80 (WARNING). The custom thresholds 90/80 are used instead of defaults 70/50

test_cases.title: Serializable isolation prevents exceeding MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY=50 on concurrent creates
steps: Create 49 active goals for a company (one below MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY=50)
Simultaneously send 5 POST requests to /api/finance/goals
Each request uses Serializable transaction with count+create
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 1 of the 5 concurrent requests succeeds (reaching 50), the other 4 fail with HTTP 400 { error: 'Maximum of 50 active goals reached' }. The Serializable isolation level (Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel.Serializable) prevents TOCTOU race on the count check by serializing concurrent transactions

test_cases.title: Create exactly 50 goals (at the MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY limit)
steps: Create 49 active non-archived goals for a company
Create 1 more goal via POST /api/finance/goals
test_cases.expected_result: 50th goal is created successfully. The count check is `if (activeGoalCount >= MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY)` where MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY=50. With 49 existing, the count returns 49, which is NOT >= 50, so the create proceeds

test_cases.title: 51st goal rejected when MAX_GOALS_PER_COMPANY limit reached
steps: Create 50 active non-archived goals for a company
Attempt to create a 51st goal via POST /api/finance/goals
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 with body { error: 'Maximum of 50 active goals reached' }. The transaction throws Error('MAX_GOALS'), caught by the outer catch which checks msg === 'MAX_GOALS' and returns the formatted 400 response

test_cases.title: Goal name validation enforces z.string().min(1).max(200)
steps: POST /api/finance/goals with name='' (empty string)
POST /api/finance/goals with name of 201 characters
POST /api/finance/goals with name of exactly 200 characters
POST /api/finance/goals with name of exactly 1 character
test_cases.expected_result: Empty name returns HTTP 400 { error: 'Validation failed', details: { name: [...] } }. 201-char name returns the same. 1-char and 200-char names succeed. Validated by createGoalSchema: name: z.string().min(1).max(200)

test_cases.title: Goal targetValue must be positive in API route but allows zero in server action
steps: POST /api/finance/goals with targetValue=-100 (API route uses z.number().positive())
POST /api/finance/goals with targetValue=0 (API route uses z.number().positive())
Call createGoal server action with targetValue=0 (server action uses goalFormDataSchema with z.number().min(0))
Call createGoal server action with targetValue=-100
test_cases.expected_result: API route: both -100 and 0 rejected with HTTP 400 validation error (z.number().positive() rejects <= 0). Server action: 0 accepted (goalFormDataSchema uses z.number().min(0)), -100 rejected. This is a known inconsistency between the two creation paths

test_cases.title: warningThreshold must be >= criticalThreshold via Zod refine
steps: POST /api/finance/goals with warningThreshold=40 and criticalThreshold=60
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 { error: 'Validation failed', details: { warningThreshold: ['warningThreshold must be >= criticalThreshold'] } }. The .refine(d => d.warningThreshold >= d.criticalThreshold, { message: '...', path: ['warningThreshold'] }) rejects this because 40 < 60

test_cases.title: endDate must be >= startDate via Zod refine
steps: POST /api/finance/goals with startDate='2026-03-15T00:00:00.000Z' and endDate='2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z'
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 { error: 'Validation failed', details: { endDate: ['endDate must be >= startDate'] } }. The .refine(d => new Date(d.endDate) >= new Date(d.startDate), { message: '...', path: ['endDate'] }) rejects this

test_cases.title: Payload exceeding MAX_GOAL_PAYLOAD_BYTES (102400) returns HTTP 413
steps: Send POST to /api/finance/goals with Content-Length header set to 110000 (> 102400 bytes)
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 413 { error: 'Payload too large' }. The code checks: const contentLength = parseInt(request.headers.get('content-length') || '0', 10); if (contentLength > MAX_GOAL_PAYLOAD_BYTES) return 413. MAX_GOAL_PAYLOAD_BYTES = 102_400 (100 KB)

test_cases.title: Invalid JSON body returns HTTP 400 with 'Invalid JSON body'
steps: Send POST to /api/finance/goals with body 'not valid json{{'
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 { error: 'Invalid JSON body' }. The code wraps request.json() in try/catch: try { raw = await request.json(); } catch { return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }, { status: 400 }); }

test_cases.title: PATCH with endDate before existing startDate fails cross-field validation
steps: Create a goal with startDate='2026-03-01T00:00:00.000Z' and endDate='2026-03-31T00:00:00.000Z'
PATCH the goal with only endDate='2026-02-15T00:00:00.000Z' (before the existing startDate)
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 with validation error. The API merges the existing goal's startDate with the new endDate for cross-field validation, detecting that mergedEnd (2026-02-15) < mergedStart (2026-03-01)

test_cases.title: Goal with targetValue=0 results in progressPercent=0
steps: Create a goal with targetValue=0 via the createGoal server action (goalFormDataSchema allows z.number().min(0))
Generate some data matching the metric so currentValue > 0
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: progressPercent = 0. The code checks: targetValue > 0 ? Math.round((currentValue / targetValue) * 100) : 0. Since targetValue=0, the ternary returns 0. Status is 'CRITICAL' because 0 < criticalThreshold (default 50)

test_cases.title: Goal with equal startDate and endDate (single day) has totalDays=1
steps: Create a goal with startDate=2026-02-15T00:00:00Z and endDate=2026-02-15T00:00:00Z
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: Goal functions correctly. totalDays = Math.max(1, Math.ceil((endDate - startDate) / 86400000)) = Math.max(1, 0) = 1. This prevents division by zero in projectedValue calculation (dailyRate * totalDays where totalDays is guaranteed >= 1)

test_cases.title: Equal warningThreshold and criticalThreshold collapses WARNING range
steps: Create a goal with warningThreshold=60 and criticalThreshold=60
Generate data so progressPercent = exactly 60
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: Validation passes (60 >= 60 satisfies the refine check). Status is 'ON_TRACK' because 60 >= warningThreshold (60). The WARNING range (>= criticalThreshold AND < warningThreshold) is effectively empty when both are equal, making it impossible to be in WARNING state

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation on all goal queries via where clause
steps: Create goals for companyId=1 and companyId=2
Authenticate as user from companyId=1
Call getGoalsWithProgress()
test_cases.expected_result: Only companyId=1 goals are returned. getGoalsForCompanyInternal queries with { companyId, isArchived: false } in the where clause. calculateMetricValue also receives companyId and includes it in every metric query's where clause

test_cases.title: canViewGoals permission required for all goal operations
steps: Authenticate as a user without canViewGoals permission
Attempt GET /api/finance/goals -- checks hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewGoals')
Attempt POST /api/finance/goals -- checks both canViewFinance AND canViewGoals
Attempt createGoal server action -- calls requireGoalUser which checks hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewGoals')
test_cases.expected_result: API routes return HTTP 403 { error: 'Forbidden' }. Server actions throw Error('Forbidden'). The GET route checks canViewFinance first, then canViewGoals. The POST route checks both. Server action requireGoalUser checks canViewGoals

test_cases.title: Rate limiting enforced: goalMutation 30/min, goalPreview 20/min, goalRead 60/min
steps: Authenticate as a valid user
Send 31 POST requests to /api/finance/goals within 60 seconds
Send 21 previewGoalValue calls within 60 seconds
Send 61 GET requests to /api/finance/goals within 60 seconds
test_cases.expected_result: 31st mutation returns rate limit error (RATE_LIMITS.goalMutation: { prefix: 'goal-mut', max: 30, windowSeconds: 60 }). 21st preview throws 'Rate limit exceeded' (RATE_LIMITS.goalPreview: { prefix: 'goal-prev', max: 20, windowSeconds: 60 }). 61st read returns rate limit error (RATE_LIMITS.goalRead: { prefix: 'goal-read', max: 60, windowSeconds: 60 })

test_cases.title: getGoalsForCompany rejects cross-company access
steps: Authenticate as user from companyId=1
Call getGoalsForCompany(companyId=2)
test_cases.expected_result: Throws Error('Forbidden'). The function checks: if (user.companyId !== companyId) throw new Error('Forbidden'). This prevents horizontal privilege escalation where a user tries to access another company's goals by passing a different companyId parameter

test_cases.title: Invalid tableId from different company rejected in goal creation
steps: Authenticate as user from companyId=1
Create a TableMeta belonging to companyId=2 with id=99
POST to /api/finance/goals with tableId=99 in the body
test_cases.expected_result: Returns HTTP 400 { error: 'Invalid tableId' }. Inside the Serializable transaction, tx.tableMeta.findFirst({ where: { id: 99, companyId: user.companyId=1 } }) returns null (table belongs to company 2), throwing Error('INVALID_TABLE'), which is caught and mapped to the 400 response

test_cases.title: Goal computation triggered when revenue data changes via finance record creation
steps: Create a REVENUE goal with targetType='SUM', targetValue=10000 for the current month
Add a FinanceRecord with type='INCOME', amount=5000 via addFinanceRecord
Call getGoalsWithProgress() and check the goal's currentValue includes the new record
test_cases.expected_result: The goal's currentValue includes the 5000 from the new FinanceRecord. getGoalsWithProgress calls getGoalsForCompanyInternal which calls enrichGoalsWithProgress, which calls calculateMetricValue for each goal. The REVENUE case with source='FINANCE_RECORD' aggregates the new record. If a stale cache exists, the function triggers 'dashboard/refresh-goals' Inngest event for background recomputation

test_cases.title: enrichGoalsWithProgress uses batched concurrent queries with CONCURRENCY_LIMIT=5
steps: Create 20 active goals with various metricTypes for one company
Call getGoalsWithProgress()
test_cases.expected_result: The function groups goals by goalBatchKey, separates shared (RETAINERS, TASKS REDUCE) from individual goals, then processes both types via runChunked with CONCURRENCY_LIMIT=5. This means at most 5 concurrent calculateMetricValue calls run in parallel per chunk, preventing database connection pool exhaustion while maximizing throughput

test_cases.title: Shared metric groups (RETAINERS) compute once for multiple goals
steps: Create 5 goals all with metricType='RETAINERS' and same filters (same clientId, frequency, targetType)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: calculateMetricValue is called only ONCE for all 5 goals because they share the same goalBatchKey (e.g., 'RET|0|all|COUNT'). The result is stored in valueMap and reused: `for (const goal of groupGoals) valueMap.set(goal.id, val)`. This reduces database queries from 5 to 1

test_cases.title: Archive snapshot preserves goal progress at time of archiving
steps: Create a REVENUE goal with targetValue=10000
Generate revenue data to reach currentValue=8000
Archive the goal via toggleGoalArchive(goalId, true)
Add more revenue data (5000 more)
Call getArchivedGoals() and verify the snapshot still shows 8000
test_cases.expected_result: The archived goal shows currentValue=8000 from the stored _archivedSnapshot, NOT the current live value of 13000. This proves the snapshot captures a point-in-time view. The archivedAt ISO timestamp records when the snapshot was taken

test_cases.title: Goal recommendation text adapts based on progress and metricType
steps: Create a RETAINERS goal at 50% progress
Create a REVENUE goal at 50% progress with 10 days remaining and 5000 gap
Create a goal at 85% progress (any type)
Create a goal at 100% progress (any type)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress for all
test_cases.expected_result: Recommendations from generateRecommendation: (1) RETAINERS at 50%: returns Hebrew text with gap amount for retainer increase. (2) REVENUE at 50%: returns daily pace needed (gap/daysRemaining units per day). (3) At 85% (>= 80): returns 'Almost there, keep going!' message. (4) At 100% (gap <= 0): returns 'Goal achieved!' message with emoji

test_cases.title: getGoalsForCompanyInternal falls back to live computation when Redis is down
steps: Configure Redis to be unavailable (simulating cache failure)
Call getGoalsForCompanyInternal(companyId)
test_cases.expected_result: The function wraps getCachedGoals in a try/catch that catches any Redis errors and falls through to live computation. The comment states '// Redis down -- fall through to live computation'. Goals are fetched from prisma.goal.findMany and enriched via enrichGoalsWithProgress, ensuring the feature works even without cache

test_cases.title: REDUCE mode progress calculation: currentValue at or below targetValue = 100%
steps: Create a TASKS REDUCE goal with targetValue=5
Ensure there are exactly 3 tasks with status='todo' (currentValue=3, which is <= targetValue=5)
Call enrichGoalsWithProgress
test_cases.expected_result: progressPercent = 100. In REDUCE mode, the first branch checks: if (currentValue <= targetValue) progressPercent = 100. Since 3 <= 5, progress is 100% meaning the reduce target has been met (fewer tasks remaining than the goal target)

test_cases.title: Create a new meeting type with valid fields via createMeetingType server action
steps: Authenticate as user with canManageMeetings permission flag in permissions JSON

Call createMeetingType({ name: 'Discovery Call', description: 'Initial consultation', slug: 'discovery-call', duration: 30, bufferBefore: 5, bufferAfter: 5, minAdvanceHours: 24, maxAdvanceDays: 30, customFields: [], isActive: true, order: 0 })

Verify response has { success: true, data: { id, companyId, name: 'Discovery Call', slug: 'discovery-call', duration: 30, bufferBefore: 5, bufferAfter: 5, minAdvanceHours: 24, maxAdvanceDays: 30, shareToken, isActive: true } }
Verify shareToken is a non-empty cuid string (unique constraint in schema)
test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: true } with a MeetingType object containing all input fields, a unique shareToken (cuid), and companyId matching the authenticated user's company

test_cases.title: Book a meeting through POST /api/p/meetings/[token]/book public endpoint
steps: Create a MeetingType with shareToken matching /^[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,50}$/ and isActive=true
Configure CompanyAvailability with weeklySchedule having a window on the target day
Send POST /api/p/meetings/{shareToken}/book with body { participantName: 'John Doe', participantEmail: 'client@example.com', startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }
Verify response is { success: true, manageToken: '<cuid>' }
Verify in database: Meeting created with status PENDING, CalendarEvent created with title '{meetingType.name} - John Doe', Client found or created by email
Verify all three records (Meeting, CalendarEvent, Client) were created atomically inside prisma.$transaction
test_cases.expected_result: POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book returns { success: true, manageToken } with Meeting (status=PENDING, calendarEventId set, clientId set), CalendarEvent (title='{name} - John Doe'), and Client (email='client@example.com') all created in a single transaction

test_cases.title: Confirm a pending meeting via updateMeetingStatus
steps: Create a meeting with status PENDING via public booking
Authenticate as user with canManageMeetings permission
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'CONFIRMED')
Verify response is { success: true, data: { status: 'CONFIRMED' } }
Verify notification is created for admin users with title containing 'מאושר'
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingStatus returns { success: true } with status=CONFIRMED, and admin notification is sent with status label 'מאושר'

test_cases.title: Complete a confirmed meeting via updateMeetingStatus
steps: Create a meeting with status CONFIRMED
Authenticate as user with canManageMeetings permission
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'COMPLETED')
Verify response is { success: true, data: { status: 'COMPLETED' } }
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingStatus transitions from CONFIRMED to COMPLETED successfully and returns { success: true }

test_cases.title: Cancel a confirmed meeting via cancelMeeting with reason
steps: Create a meeting with status CONFIRMED
Authenticate as user with canManageMeetings permission
Call cancelMeeting(meetingId, 'Customer requested cancellation')
Verify response is { success: true, data: { status: 'CANCELLED', cancelledAt: <Date>, cancelledBy: 'owner', cancelReason: 'Customer requested cancellation' } }
Verify fireMeetingAutomations is called with triggerType='MEETING_CANCELLED'
Verify the time slot is freed in subsequent getAvailableSlots call
test_cases.expected_result: cancelMeeting sets status=CANCELLED, cancelledAt=now, cancelledBy='owner', cancelReason truncated to 1000 chars (MAX_CANCEL_REASON_LENGTH), fires MEETING_CANCELLED automations, and frees the slot

test_cases.title: Reschedule a meeting updates both Meeting and CalendarEvent atomically
steps: Create a confirmed meeting at 2026-03-10T10:00:00Z with a linked CalendarEvent via public booking
Authenticate as user with canManageMeetings permission
Call rescheduleMeeting(meetingId, '2026-03-11T14:00:00Z', '2026-03-11T14:30:00Z')
Verify response is { success: true, data: { startTime: '2026-03-11T14:00:00Z', endTime: '2026-03-11T14:30:00Z' } }
Query CalendarEvent by meeting.calendarEventId and verify startTime and endTime match the new values
Call getAvailableSlots for 2026-03-10 and verify 10:00 slot is now available
test_cases.expected_result: rescheduleMeeting updates Meeting.startTime/endTime and the linked CalendarEvent.startTime/endTime to the new times; the old slot (2026-03-10T10:00) is freed

test_cases.title: getAvailableSlots generates correct slot count from weeklySchedule
steps: Configure CompanyAvailability weeklySchedule: { '0': [{ start: '09:00', end: '17:00' }], '1': [{ start: '10:00', end: '15:00' }] } timezone='Asia/Jerusalem'
Set MeetingType duration=30, bufferBefore=0, bufferAfter=0, dailyLimit=null
Call getAvailableSlots for a date range containing a Sunday and Monday with no existing meetings
Count slots per day
test_cases.expected_result: Sunday generates 16 slots (09:00, 09:30, ... 16:30) and Monday generates 10 slots (10:00, 10:30, ... 14:30) in the SlotsByDate result keyed by date string 'YYYY-MM-DD'

test_cases.title: AvailabilityBlock (blocked date) excluded from slot generation
steps: Configure weeklySchedule with Monday-Friday 09:00-17:00
Create AvailabilityBlock with startDate=2026-03-15T00:00:00Z, endDate=2026-03-16T00:00:00Z, allDay=true (blocking March 15)
Call getAvailableSlots with blocks array containing this block for the week of 2026-03-15
Verify no slots returned for the date key '2026-03-15'
test_cases.expected_result: The day covered by the AvailabilityBlock is skipped in slot generation (isDayBlocked check: block.start <= dayStart && block.end >= dayEnd); other weekdays return normal slots

test_cases.title: Buffer time reduces available slots via occupiedStart/occupiedEnd calculation
steps: Configure weeklySchedule Monday 10:00-11:00 in Asia/Jerusalem
Set MeetingType duration=30, bufferBefore=10, bufferAfter=10
Create an existing meeting at 10:00-10:30 with bufferBefore=10, bufferAfter=10 (occupies 09:50-10:40)
Call getAvailableSlots for that Monday
Verify hasOverlap(busyIntervals, occupiedStart=10:20, occupiedEnd=11:10) returns true for the 10:30 slot (its occupiedStart=10:20 overlaps busy interval ending at 10:40)
test_cases.expected_result: Only 0 slots available: the 10:00 slot is booked and its buffer (until 10:40) causes hasOverlap to return true for the 10:30 slot (occupiedStart=10:20 < busyEnd=10:40)

test_cases.title: Daily meeting limit enforced in getAvailableSlots
steps: Configure MeetingType with dailyLimit=3
Set meetingsPerDay['2026-03-10']=3 in the getAvailableSlots params
Call getAvailableSlots for 2026-03-10
Verify no slots are returned for '2026-03-10' because dayCount(3) >= dailyLimit(3) triggers continue
test_cases.expected_result: getAvailableSlots returns {} for the date when meetingsPerDay[dateStr] >= dailyLimit, skipping the entire day in the slot generation loop

test_cases.title: minAdvanceHours filters out too-soon slots
steps: Configure MeetingType with minAdvanceHours=24 (default)
Mock Date.now() to 2026-03-10T10:00:00Z
Call getAvailableSlots
Verify earliestBookable = now + 24*60*60*1000 = 2026-03-11T10:00:00Z
Verify all returned slots have start >= 2026-03-11T10:00:00Z
test_cases.expected_result: All returned slots satisfy slotStart >= earliestBookable (now + minAdvanceHours * 3600000); no slots from 2026-03-10 or 2026-03-11 before 10:00 appear

test_cases.title: maxAdvanceDays limits how far in the future slots are offered
steps: Configure MeetingType with maxAdvanceDays=14
Mock Date.now() to 2026-03-01T10:00:00Z
Call getAvailableSlots with dateEnd=2026-04-01
Verify maxDate = now + 14 days = 2026-03-15T10:00:00Z
Verify effectiveEnd = min(dateEnd, maxDate) = 2026-03-15T10:00:00Z
Verify no slots after 2026-03-15 are returned
test_cases.expected_result: getAvailableSlots clamps effectiveEnd to now + maxAdvanceDays; no slots beyond that date appear in the result

test_cases.title: hasOverlap uses O(log n) binary search on sorted merged intervals
steps: Generate 1000 non-overlapping busy intervals: [{start: i*3600000, end: i*3600000+1800000}] for i=0..999
Call hasOverlap(sorted, start=500*3600000+100, end=500*3600000+200) targeting interval #500
Verify result is true
Measure iterations: binary search should complete in ~10 iterations (log2(1000)), not 1000
test_cases.expected_result: hasOverlap returns true and completes in O(log n) iterations (~10 for n=1000) using the binary search loop (lo/hi with mid = (lo+hi)>>1)

test_cases.title: hasOverlap returns false for adjacent non-overlapping intervals
steps: Create sorted busy intervals: [{ start: 1000, end: 2000 }]
Call hasOverlap(sorted, 2000, 3000) -- adjacent after (start equals busy.end)
Verify returns false because sorted[mid].end(2000) <= start(2000) triggers lo = mid + 1
Call hasOverlap(sorted, 0, 1000) -- adjacent before (end equals busy.start)
Verify returns false because sorted[mid].start(1000) >= end(1000) triggers hi = mid - 1
test_cases.expected_result: hasOverlap returns false for both adjacent ranges: the boundary condition sorted[mid].end <= start and sorted[mid].start >= end correctly excludes touching-but-not-overlapping intervals

test_cases.title: Double-booking prevented by re-verification inside prisma.$transaction
steps: Configure a single available slot at 2026-03-10T10:00
Booking A: POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with startTime='2026-03-10T10:00:00Z'
Before A's transaction completes, Booking B: POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with same startTime
Booking B commits first inside its $transaction, creating Meeting+CalendarEvent
Booking A enters $transaction, re-queries tx.meeting.findMany with startTime/endTime overlap filter
isSlotAvailable returns false inside A's transaction because B's meeting now exists
A throws Error('SLOT_TAKEN')
test_cases.expected_result: The re-verification inside prisma.$transaction (lines 96-127 of book/route.ts) detects B's committed meeting and throws 'SLOT_TAKEN', returning { error: 'Slot is no longer available' } with status 400

test_cases.title: 5 concurrent bookings for same slot: exactly 1 succeeds
steps: Configure a single available slot at 2026-03-10T14:00
Fire 5 concurrent POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book requests from 5 different IPs with same startTime
Await all 5 responses
Count successes (HTTP 200 with { success: true }) and failures (HTTP 400 with { error: 'Slot is no longer available' })
Query database for Meeting count at that startTime
test_cases.expected_result: Exactly 1 request returns { success: true, manageToken }, 4 return { error: 'Slot is no longer available' } (400), and database contains exactly 1 Meeting and 1 CalendarEvent for that slot

test_cases.title: Slot times in Asia/Jerusalem correctly converted to UTC via getTzOffsetMs
steps: Configure weeklySchedule Monday 09:00-12:00 timezone='Asia/Jerusalem'
Call getAvailableSlots for a Monday in January 2026 (UTC+2, standard time)
Verify getTzOffsetMs returns +2h = 7200000ms
Verify parseTimeToDate computes UTC = localTime - offset: 09:00 local -> 07:00 UTC
Verify returned slot.start for first slot equals 2026-01-05T07:00:00.000Z
test_cases.expected_result: Slots are computed in Asia/Jerusalem local time and stored as UTC; 09:00 local = 07:00 UTC during standard time (UTC+2), verified via parseTimeToDate(day, '09:00', 7200000)

test_cases.title: DST spring-forward in Asia/Jerusalem: skipped hour handled
steps: Configure weeklySchedule for the DST transition day (March 27, 2026 in Israel: clocks jump from 02:00 to 03:00)
Set availability window 01:00-04:00
Call getAvailableSlots for 2026-03-27
Verify getTzOffsetMs returns +2h before 02:00 and +3h after 03:00
Verify no duplicate or invalid slots are generated for the 02:00-03:00 gap
test_cases.expected_result: Slot generation handles DST spring-forward correctly: the skipped hour does not produce duplicate or phantom slots because parseTimeToDate uses the offset at the day boundary

test_cases.title: UTC conversion differs across DST boundary dates
steps: Slot on 2026-03-20T14:00 Asia/Jerusalem (before DST, UTC+2): expected UTC = 12:00
Slot on 2026-03-27T14:00 Asia/Jerusalem (after DST, UTC+3): expected UTC = 11:00
Call getTzOffsetMs('Asia/Jerusalem', new Date('2026-03-20T00:00:00Z')) -> 7200000 (2h)
Call getTzOffsetMs('Asia/Jerusalem', new Date('2026-03-27T00:00:00Z')) -> 10800000 (3h)
Verify parseTimeToDate(day, '14:00', 7200000).toISOString() === '2026-03-20T12:00:00.000Z'
Verify parseTimeToDate(day, '14:00', 10800000).toISOString() === '2026-03-27T11:00:00.000Z'
test_cases.expected_result: getTzOffsetMs correctly returns 7200000ms (UTC+2) before DST and 10800000ms (UTC+3) after DST; parseTimeToDate produces correct UTC times accordingly

test_cases.title: GET /api/p/meetings/[token] returns meeting type details for valid token
steps: Create MeetingType with isActive=true, name='Demo', duration=30, customFields=[{label:'Company',type:'text'}]
Note the generated shareToken (cuid matching /^[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,50}$/)
Send GET /api/p/meetings/{shareToken}
Verify response includes { id, name: 'Demo', duration: 30, customFields, company: { name, logoUrl }, availableDays: [0,1,2,3,4] }
test_cases.expected_result: GET /api/p/meetings/{token} returns meeting type with id, name, description, duration, color, customFields, minAdvanceHours, maxAdvanceDays, company.name, company.logoUrl, and availableDays array derived from weeklySchedule

test_cases.title: GET /api/p/meetings/[token] rejects tokens not matching /^[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,50}$/
steps: Send GET /api/p/meetings/short (9 chars) -> expect 400 { error: 'Invalid token' }
Send GET /api/p/meetings/invalid!@#token (special chars) -> expect 400 { error: 'Invalid token' }
Send GET /api/p/meetings/{51-char-alphanumeric-string} (exceeds 50) -> expect 400 { error: 'Invalid token' }
Verify TOKEN_RE = /^[a-zA-Z0-9]{10,50}$/ is applied before any DB query
test_cases.expected_result: All three requests return 400 with { error: 'Invalid token' }; no database query is executed for invalid tokens

test_cases.title: Public booking rate limited: 10/IP/min via RATE_LIMITS.publicBooking
steps: Send 10 POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book requests from IP 1.2.3.4 within 60 seconds
Verify all 10 return 200
Send 11th request from same IP within same 60-second window
Verify response is 429 Too Many Requests (checkRateLimit returns NextResponse with 429)
test_cases.expected_result: RATE_LIMITS.publicBooking = { prefix: 'pub-book', max: 10, windowSeconds: 60 }; 11th request from same IP returns 429

test_cases.title: Public booking creates new Client when email not found in company
steps: Ensure no Client with email 'newclient@example.com' exists for the company
POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with { participantName: 'New Client', participantEmail: 'newclient@example.com', startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }
Verify inside $transaction: tx.client.findFirst returns null, then tx.client.create is called
Verify Meeting.clientId references the newly created Client
test_cases.expected_result: A new Client record is created within the booking transaction with { companyId, name: 'New Client', email: 'newclient@example.com', phone: null } and linked to the Meeting via clientId

test_cases.title: Public booking reuses existing Client found by email
steps: Create Client with email='existing@example.com' and companyId matching the MeetingType's company
POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with { participantName: 'Existing Client', participantEmail: 'existing@example.com', startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }
Verify tx.client.findFirst({ where: { companyId, OR: [{ email: 'existing@example.com' }] } }) returns existing client
Verify no new Client is created and Meeting.clientId equals existing client's id
test_cases.expected_result: Existing client found by email within the transaction is reused; Meeting.clientId references the pre-existing Client, no duplicate created

test_cases.title: Public booking rejects request with neither email nor phone
steps: POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with { participantName: 'John Doe', startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' } (no email, no phone)
Verify validateBookingInput returns { valid: false, error: 'נדרש אימייל או טלפון' }
Verify response is 400 { error: 'נדרש אימייל או טלפון' }
test_cases.expected_result: POST returns 400 with { error: 'נדרש אימייל או טלפון' } because validateBookingInput checks (!email && !phone) and returns validation error

test_cases.title: Public booking succeeds with phone only (no email)
steps: POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book with { participantName: 'Jane Doe', participantPhone: '+972501234567', startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }
Verify validateBookingInput passes: phone matches PHONE_REGEX /^[\d\s\-+().]{7,20}$/
Verify response is { success: true, manageToken: '<cuid>' }
Verify Client created with phone='+972501234567', email=null
test_cases.expected_result: Booking succeeds with phone only; Client created with phone set and email null; Meeting linked to new Client

test_cases.title: createMeetingType rejects 51st meeting type (MAX_MEETING_TYPES_PER_COMPANY=50)
steps: Create 50 meeting types for the company
Call createMeetingType with valid data for the 51st type
Verify prisma.meetingType.count returns 50 >= MAX_MEETING_TYPES_PER_COMPANY(50)
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'מקסימום 50 סוגי פגישות' }
test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: false, error: 'מקסימום 50 סוגי פגישות' } because count(50) >= MAX_MEETING_TYPES_PER_COMPANY(50)

test_cases.title: Public reschedule via POST /api/p/meetings/manage/[manageToken]/reschedule updates Meeting and CalendarEvent atomically
steps: Create a confirmed meeting with manageToken and linked calendarEventId
POST /api/p/meetings/manage/{manageToken}/reschedule with { startTime: '2026-03-12T15:00:00Z' }
Verify inside $transaction: slot re-verified via isSlotAvailable, tx.meeting.update sets new startTime/endTime, tx.calendarEvent.update sets matching times
Verify response is { success: true }
test_cases.expected_result: POST returns { success: true }; both Meeting and CalendarEvent are updated to the new startTime and endTime (startTime + duration) atomically within a $transaction, with TOCTOU protection via isSlotAvailable inside the transaction

test_cases.title: Reschedule frees old slot for new bookings
steps: Book all available slots on 2026-03-10 (fully booked day)
Reschedule one meeting from 2026-03-10T10:00 to 2026-03-11T10:00 via rescheduleMeeting
Call getAvailableSlots for 2026-03-10 with updated existingMeetings (minus the rescheduled one)
Verify the 10:00 slot appears in the available slots
test_cases.expected_result: After rescheduling, the 10:00 slot on 2026-03-10 is no longer in existingMeetings and hasOverlap returns false for it, making it available again

test_cases.title: Reschedule to occupied slot returns 'Slot is no longer available'
steps: Book meeting A at 2026-03-10T10:00
Book meeting B at 2026-03-10T14:00
POST /api/p/meetings/manage/{meetingB.manageToken}/reschedule with { startTime: '2026-03-10T10:00:00Z' }
Verify isSlotAvailable returns false inside $transaction (meeting A's time range overlaps)
Verify response is 400 { error: 'Slot is no longer available' }
test_cases.expected_result: Reschedule returns 400 with { error: 'Slot is no longer available' } because isSlotAvailable detects meeting A occupies the target slot inside the transaction

test_cases.title: Meeting type name exceeding MAX_NAME_LENGTH=200 rejected

test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: false, error: 'שם לא יכול לעלות על 200 תווים' } because name.trim().length(201) > MAX_NAME_LENGTH(200)

test_cases.title: Meeting type description exceeding MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH=2000 rejected

Verify validateMeetingTypeInput returns { valid: false, error: 'תיאור לא יכול לעלות על 2000 תווים' }
test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: false, error: 'תיאור לא יכול לעלות על 2000 תווים' } because description.length(2001) > MAX_DESCRIPTION_LENGTH(2000)

test_cases.title: Meeting type slug exceeding MAX_SLUG_LENGTH=100 rejected
steps: Call createMeetingType({ name: 'Test', slug: 'a'.repeat(101), duration: 30 })
Verify validateMeetingTypeInput returns { valid: false, error: 'slug לא יכול לעלות על 100 תווים' }
test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: false, error: 'slug לא יכול לעלות על 100 תווים' } because slug.length(101) > MAX_SLUG_LENGTH(100)

test_cases.title: Custom fields exceeding MAX_CUSTOM_FIELDS=20 rejected

test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType returns { success: false, error: 'מקסימום 20 שדות מותאמים' } because customFields.length(21) > MAX_CUSTOM_FIELDS(20)

test_cases.title: Tags exceeding limits rejected by validateTags
steps: Call updateMeetingTags(meetingId, Array(21).fill('tag'))
Verify validateTags returns null for 21 tags (> MAX_TAGS=20)
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'תגיות לא תקינות' }
Call updateMeetingTags(meetingId, ['a'.repeat(51)])
Verify validateTags returns null for tag with length 51 (> MAX_TAG_LENGTH=50)
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'תגיות לא תקינות' }
test_cases.expected_result: Both calls return { success: false, error: 'תגיות לא תקינות' } because validateTags returns null when tags.length > 20 or any tag.length > 50

test_cases.title: Notes exceeding MAX_NOTES_LENGTH=5000 rejected
steps: Call updateMeetingNotes(meetingId, 'A'.repeat(5001), undefined)
Verify validateNotes returns null for string length 5001 > MAX_NOTES_LENGTH(5000)
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'הערות לפני ארוכות מדי' }
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingNotes returns { success: false, error: 'הערות לפני ארוכות מדי' } because validateNotes returns null when notes.length > MAX_NOTES_LENGTH(5000)

test_cases.title: updateMeetingStatus accepts all valid statuses without FSM enforcement
steps: Create a meeting with status PENDING
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'COMPLETED')
Verify the status is updated (note: updateMeetingStatus validates status is in validStatuses=['PENDING','CONFIRMED','COMPLETED','CANCELLED','NO_SHOW'] but does NOT enforce state machine transitions)
This documents actual codebase behavior: no transition validation exists
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingStatus accepts any valid status string from validStatuses array without enforcing state machine transitions; PENDING->COMPLETED is allowed by the current implementation (no fromStatus check exists in the code)

test_cases.title: updateMeetingStatus rejects invalid status string
steps: Create a meeting with status COMPLETED
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'INVALID_STATUS')
Verify 'INVALID_STATUS' is not in validStatuses=['PENDING','CONFIRMED','COMPLETED','CANCELLED','NO_SHOW']
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'סטטוס לא תקין' }
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingStatus returns { success: false, error: 'סטטוס לא תקין' } because 'INVALID_STATUS' is not in the validStatuses array

test_cases.title: Mark meeting as NO_SHOW via updateMeetingStatus
steps: Create a meeting with status CONFIRMED
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'NO_SHOW')
Verify response is { success: true, data: { status: 'NO_SHOW' } }
Verify notification sent to admins with label 'לא הגיע'
test_cases.expected_result: updateMeetingStatus returns { success: true } with status='NO_SHOW' and notification title contains 'לא הגיע' from statusLabels map

test_cases.title: Cancel a PENDING meeting directly via cancelMeeting
steps: Create a meeting with status PENDING
Call cancelMeeting(meetingId, 'No longer needed')
Verify response contains { success: true, data: { status: 'CANCELLED', cancelledBy: 'owner' } }
test_cases.expected_result: cancelMeeting succeeds from PENDING status; sets status=CANCELLED, cancelledAt=now(), cancelledBy='owner', cancelReason='No longer needed'

test_cases.title: Concurrent bookings for different slots all succeed
steps: Configure availability with 5 distinct slots: 10:00, 10:30, 11:00, 11:30, 12:00 on 2026-03-10
Fire 5 concurrent POST /api/p/meetings/{token}/book requests, each targeting a different slot
Await all responses
Verify all 5 return { success: true, manageToken } because isSlotAvailable returns true for each distinct slot
Verify 5 Meeting records and 5 CalendarEvent records exist
test_cases.expected_result: All 5 bookings for distinct time slots succeed because the $transaction re-verification finds no overlap for each unique slot; 5 Meetings and 5 CalendarEvents created

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation prevents cross-company meeting access via getMeetings
steps: Create a meeting in Company A (companyId=1)
Authenticate as user in Company B (companyId=2) with canViewMeetings permission
Call getMeetings() which queries where: { companyId: user.companyId(=2) }
Verify Company A's meeting does not appear in results
test_cases.expected_result: getMeetings filters by user.companyId; meetings from other companies are never returned because the WHERE clause always includes companyId filter

test_cases.title: getMeetings requires canViewMeetings permission flag
steps: Authenticate as user without canViewMeetings in permissions JSON
Call getMeetings()
Verify hasUserFlag(user, 'canViewMeetings') returns false
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' }
test_cases.expected_result: getMeetings returns { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' } for user lacking canViewMeetings permission flag

test_cases.title: Write operations require canManageMeetings permission flag
steps: Authenticate as user with canViewMeetings but WITHOUT canManageMeetings
Call createMeetingType({ name: 'Test', slug: 'test', duration: 30 })
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' }
Call updateMeetingStatus(meetingId, 'CONFIRMED')
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' }
Call cancelMeeting(meetingId, 'test')
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' }
test_cases.expected_result: All write operations (createMeetingType, updateMeetingStatus, cancelMeeting, rescheduleMeeting, updateMeetingNotes, updateMeetingTags, deleteMeetingType) return { success: false, error: 'Forbidden' } for users without canManageMeetings

test_cases.title: Slot at midnight boundary (23:30-00:00) handled by getAvailableSlots
steps: Configure weeklySchedule with window ending at 00:00 (next day midnight equivalent: '00:00' parsed as 0 hours)
Set duration=30, verify parseTimeToDate converts '23:30' and '00:00' correctly
Note: '00:00' as end time means the window goes to midnight of the SAME day (start of day), which would make windowEnd <= windowStart, producing zero slots
This test documents the actual behavior when end='00:00'
test_cases.expected_result: When window end is '00:00', parseTimeToDate produces a time at the start of the day (00:00), which is before any afternoon slot start; the while loop condition (slotStart + duration <= windowEnd) is false immediately, so no slots are generated for windows ending at '00:00'

test_cases.title: No available slots returns empty object from getAvailableSlots
steps: Configure weeklySchedule with all days having empty windows: { '0': [], '1': [], ..., '6': [] }
Call getAvailableSlots with dateStart and dateEnd spanning 14 days
Verify the inner loop skips all days (windows.length === 0 triggers continue)
Verify result is empty object {}
test_cases.expected_result: getAvailableSlots returns {} (empty SlotsByDate) with no errors when no windows are defined; the function gracefully handles zero-availability configuration

test_cases.title: MeetingType deletion cascades to AutomationRule via onDelete: Cascade
steps: Create MeetingType with id=X
Create AutomationRule with meetingTypeId=X, triggerType=MEETING_BOOKED
Verify AutomationRule exists: prisma.automationRule.findFirst({ where: { meetingTypeId: X } }) is not null
Delete MeetingType via prisma.meetingType.delete({ where: { id: X } })
Query prisma.automationRule.findFirst({ where: { meetingTypeId: X } })
test_cases.expected_result: After MeetingType deletion, the linked AutomationRule is automatically deleted due to the onDelete: Cascade on the meetingTypeId relation in the AutomationRule model (schema line: meetingType MeetingType? @relation(fields: [meetingTypeId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade))

test_cases.title: Client deletion sets Meeting.clientId to null via onDelete: SetNull
steps: Create Client with id=C
Create Meeting with clientId=C via public booking
Verify Meeting.clientId = C
Delete Client via prisma.client.delete({ where: { id: C } })
Query the Meeting and check clientId
test_cases.expected_result: After Client deletion, Meeting.clientId is set to null (not deleted) because the Meeting model defines: client Client? @relation(fields: [clientId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)

test_cases.title: Public booking triggers MEETING_BOOKED automation via fireMeetingAutomations
steps: Create MeetingType with shareToken and companyId=1
Create AutomationRule with triggerType=MEETING_BOOKED, actionType=WEBHOOK, isActive=true, companyId=1, meetingTypeId=null, meetingId=null
POST /api/p/meetings/{shareToken}/book with valid booking data
Verify booking succeeds with { success: true, manageToken }
Verify fireMeetingAutomations(companyId, 'MEETING_BOOKED', { id, meetingTypeId, participantName, ... }) is called after transaction completes

Verify fireMeetingAutomations queries rules matching: { companyId, triggerType: 'MEETING_BOOKED', isActive: true, OR: [{ meetingTypeId: null, meetingId: null }, { meetingTypeId, meetingId: null }, { meetingId }] }
Verify the matching rule's actionConfig webhook is executed via executeRuleActions
test_cases.expected_result: After a successful public booking, fireMeetingAutomations is invoked fire-and-forget with trigger 'MEETING_BOOKED'; matching active AutomationRules (global, per-type, or per-meeting) are found and their actions executed in batches of RULE_CONCURRENCY=5

test_cases.title: GET /api/p/meetings/[token] returns availableDays for client-side calendar rendering
steps: Create MeetingType with no availabilityOverride
Set CompanyAvailability weeklySchedule: { '0': [{ start: '09:00', end: '17:00' }], '1': [{ start: '09:00', end: '17:00' }], '5': [], '6': [] }
Send GET /api/p/meetings/{shareToken}
Verify response includes availableDays: [0, 1] (only days with non-empty windows)
Verify response omits companyId and availabilityOverride (internal fields)
test_cases.expected_result: The public endpoint returns availableDays array computed from weeklySchedule.entries().filter(windows.length > 0).map(day => Number(day)), enabling the client-side calendar to grey out unavailable days; internal fields (companyId, availabilityOverride) are destructured out

test_cases.title: Duplicate slug for same company returns specific Prisma P2002 error
steps: Create MeetingType with slug='intro-call' for companyId=1
Call createMeetingType({ name: 'Another', slug: 'intro-call', duration: 30 }) for same company
Verify Prisma throws error with code 'P2002' (unique constraint violation on @@unique([companyId, slug]))
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'slug כבר קיים עבור חברה זו' }
test_cases.expected_result: createMeetingType catches error.code === 'P2002' and returns { success: false, error: 'slug כבר קיים עבור חברה זו' } instead of a generic error, due to the @@unique([companyId, slug]) constraint on MeetingType

test_cases.title: deleteMeetingType soft-deletes by setting isActive=false, not hard delete
steps: Create MeetingType with id=X, isActive=true
Call deleteMeetingType(X)
Verify response is { success: true }
Query prisma.meetingType.findFirst({ where: { id: X } })
Verify record still exists with isActive=false (soft delete via update, not delete)
test_cases.expected_result: deleteMeetingType performs prisma.meetingType.update({ data: { isActive: false } }) instead of prisma.meetingType.delete; the record persists in the database with isActive=false, preserving historical data and avoiding cascade side effects

test_cases.title: Meeting deletion cascades AutomationRules linked by meetingId
steps: Create Meeting with id=M
Create AutomationRule with meetingId=M, triggerType=MEETING_REMINDER
Delete Meeting via prisma.meeting.delete({ where: { id: M } })
Query prisma.automationRule.findFirst({ where: { meetingId: M } })
test_cases.expected_result: After Meeting deletion, linked AutomationRule is cascade-deleted because AutomationRule defines: meeting Meeting? @relation(fields: [meetingId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

test_cases.title: Reschedule notification displays time in Hebrew locale with Asia/Jerusalem timezone

test_cases.title: Create a global meeting automation via createGlobalMeetingAutomation

Verify tier check: globalCount < MEETING_AUTOMATION_LIMITS['premium'](=6)
Verify response is { success: true, data: { id: <number> } }
Verify AutomationRule created with companyId, triggerType='MEETING_BOOKED', actionType='WEBHOOK', actionConfig, meetingId=null
test_cases.expected_result: createGlobalMeetingAutomation returns { success: true, data: { id } } with AutomationRule.meetingId=null (global), triggerType=MEETING_BOOKED, actionType=WEBHOOK, and name='Welcome Webhook'

test_cases.title: Update an existing global automation via updateGlobalMeetingAutomation

test_cases.expected_result: updateGlobalMeetingAutomation updates the rule's name to 'New Name', triggerType to 'MEETING_CANCELLED', and actionType to 'WEBHOOK' scoped to user's companyId

test_cases.title: Delete a global automation via deleteGlobalMeetingAutomation

test_cases.expected_result: deleteGlobalMeetingAutomation hard-deletes the AutomationRule (not soft delete) and returns { success: true }; subsequent queries return null

test_cases.title: fireMeetingAutomations executes matching rules with correct context
steps: Create AutomationRule with triggerType=MEETING_BOOKED, actionType=WEBHOOK, isActive=true, meetingTypeId=null, meetingId=null

Call fireMeetingAutomations(companyId, 'MEETING_BOOKED', { id: 'mtg1', meetingTypeId: 1, participantName: 'John', participantEmail: 'j@e.com', startTime: new Date('2026-03-10T10:00:00Z'), endTime: new Date('2026-03-10T10:30:00Z'), meetingTypeName: 'Demo' })

Verify rules queried with WHERE: { companyId, triggerType: 'MEETING_BOOKED', isActive: true, OR: [{ meetingTypeId: null, meetingId: null }, { meetingTypeId: 1, meetingId: null }, { meetingId: 'mtg1' }] }
Verify executeRuleActions called with meetingContext including meetingStart/meetingEnd in Hebrew format (formatDateHe + formatTimeHe)
test_cases.expected_result: fireMeetingAutomations finds the matching rule via the OR query, constructs meetingContext with Hebrew-formatted dates (formatDateHe/formatTimeHe with Asia/Jerusalem timezone), and calls executeRuleActions for each matching rule in batches of RULE_CONCURRENCY=5

test_cases.title: processMeetingReminders deduplicates via AutomationLog
steps: Create MEETING_REMINDER rule with triggerConfig.minutesBefore=30, isActive=true
Create meeting scheduled 20 minutes from now (within reminder window) with calendarEventId='cal1'
Run processMeetingReminders()
Verify executeRuleActions is called for the meeting
Verify AutomationLog.createMany is called with { automationRuleId: rule.id, companyId, calendarEventId: 'cal1' }
Run processMeetingReminders() again
Verify dedupSet contains 'ruleId-cal1', so the meeting is filtered out
Verify executeRuleActions is NOT called for the same meeting on second run
test_cases.expected_result: First run executes the reminder and creates an AutomationLog entry; second run finds the entry in dedupSet (from automationLog.findMany) and skips the meeting, preventing duplicate reminders. Logs are created via createMany with skipDuplicates:true in batches of LOG_BATCH_SIZE=1000

test_cases.title: Basic tier limited to 2 global meeting automations

steps: Attempt to create automation with actionConfig: { url: 'http://[::ffff:127.0.0.1]/exploit' }

test_cases.expected_result: Webhook to [::ffff:127.0.0.1] is blocked by SSRF protection

Verify SSRF protection blocks AWS/cloud metadata endpoint

Verify SSRF protection blocks Kubernetes service endpoint

test_cases.title: SSRF: Webhook with file:// scheme blocked

Verify SSRF protection rejects non-http(s) schemes
test_cases.expected_result: Webhook with file:// scheme is blocked; only http and https schemes are allowed

test_cases.title: Reminder deduplication: existing AutomationLog prevents re-execution
steps: Create MEETING_REMINDER rule (id=R) linked to meeting with calendarEventId='cal1'
Insert AutomationLog with automationRuleId=R, calendarEventId='cal1'
Run processMeetingReminders()
Verify dedupSet includes 'R-cal1'
Verify the meeting is filtered out: dedupSet.has(`${rule.id}-${m.calendarEventId}`) returns true
Verify executeRuleActions is NOT called for this meeting
test_cases.expected_result: processMeetingReminders skips the meeting because the dedupSet (built from existing AutomationLog entries) contains the ruleId-calendarEventId pair, preventing duplicate reminder execution

test_cases.title: fireMeetingAutomations processes rules in batches of RULE_CONCURRENCY=5
steps: Create 10 active automation rules matching the same trigger event
Call fireMeetingAutomations with matching trigger
Monitor the for-loop: for (let i = 0; i < rules.length; i += RULE_CONCURRENCY)
Verify first batch processes rules[0..4] via Promise.allSettled, then rules[5..9]
Verify at most 5 executeRuleActions calls are concurrent at any time
test_cases.expected_result: fireMeetingAutomations processes rules in batches of RULE_CONCURRENCY=5 using a for-loop with Promise.allSettled; first 5 rules execute concurrently, then next 5 after the first batch settles

test_cases.title: Slow rule does not block other rules within same batch (Promise.allSettled)
steps: Create 5 automation rules: 4 with fast actions (resolve in 100ms) and 1 with slow action (5000ms)
Call fireMeetingAutomations with matching trigger
Verify Promise.allSettled processes all 5 in parallel within the same batch
Verify 4 fast rules complete while slow rule is still executing
Verify all 5 results are collected (both fulfilled and potentially rejected)
test_cases.expected_result: Promise.allSettled allows all 5 rules to execute concurrently within the batch; fast rules complete independently without waiting for the slow rule. A rejected/slow rule does not affect others because allSettled never short-circuits

test_cases.title: Unmatched trigger event results in zero rules fired
steps: Create automation rule with triggerType='MEETING_BOOKED', isActive=true
Call fireMeetingAutomations(companyId, 'MEETING_CANCELLED', meetingContext)
Verify prisma.automationRule.findMany WHERE triggerType='MEETING_CANCELLED' returns empty array
Verify rules.length === 0 triggers early return
test_cases.expected_result: fireMeetingAutomations returns immediately when rules.length === 0 because no active rules match the MEETING_CANCELLED trigger type

test_cases.title: Disabled (isActive=false) automation rule excluded from firing
steps: Create automation rule with triggerType='MEETING_BOOKED', isActive=false
Call fireMeetingAutomations(companyId, 'MEETING_BOOKED', meetingContext)
Verify findMany WHERE includes isActive: true, so the disabled rule is excluded
Verify rules.length === 0 and no execution occurs
test_cases.expected_result: Disabled rules are excluded by the isActive: true filter in the findMany query; they never appear in the rules array and are never executed

test_cases.title: CompanyId isolation: automation from Company A invisible to Company B
steps: Create AutomationRule with companyId=1
Authenticate as user with companyId=2 and canViewMeetings permission
Call getGlobalMeetingAutomations()
Verify findMany WHERE companyId=2 excludes Company 1's rule
Verify response data array does not contain Company 1's rule
test_cases.expected_result: getGlobalMeetingAutomations filters by currentUser.companyId; automations from other companies are never visible or modifiable

test_cases.title: Automation mutation rate limited: RATE_LIMITS.automationMutate = 30/min
steps: Authenticate as a user
Send 30 createGlobalMeetingAutomation requests within 60 seconds
Verify all 30 pass checkActionRateLimit
Send 31st request within the same window
Verify checkActionRateLimit returns true (limited)
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'Rate limit exceeded. Please try again later.' }
test_cases.expected_result: RATE_LIMITS.automationMutate = { prefix: 'auto-mut', max: 30, windowSeconds: 60 }; 31st mutation request returns rate limit error

test_cases.title: Meeting cancellation triggers MEETING_CANCELLED automation chain
steps: Create MeetingType with companyId=1
Create AutomationRule: triggerType=MEETING_CANCELLED, actionType=SEND_NOTIFICATION, isActive=true, companyId=1, meetingTypeId=null, meetingId=null
Create AutomationRule: triggerType=MEETING_CANCELLED, actionType=CREATE_TASK, isActive=true, companyId=1, meetingTypeId=meetingType.id
Book a meeting via public endpoint
Call cancelMeeting(meetingId, 'No show') as authenticated user
Verify cancelMeeting calls fireMeetingAutomations(companyId, 'MEETING_CANCELLED', meetingPayload).catch()
Verify fireMeetingAutomations finds both rules (one global, one per-type) via OR query
Verify both rules' actions are executed via executeRuleActions
test_cases.expected_result: cancelMeeting fire-and-forgets fireMeetingAutomations which finds 2 matching rules (global + per-type) and executes both: SEND_NOTIFICATION creates a notification, CREATE_TASK creates a task, all processed in a single RULE_CONCURRENCY=5 batch

test_cases.title: fireMeetingAutomations processes 50 rules within RULE_CONCURRENCY batches
steps: Create 50 active automation rules (maximum take: 50 in findMany) matching MEETING_BOOKED trigger
Each rule has actionType=WEBHOOK with a fast endpoint (50ms response)
Call fireMeetingAutomations with matching trigger
Measure total execution time
Verify rules are processed in ceil(50/5)=10 sequential batches of 5 concurrent rules each
Expected time: ~10 * 50ms = 500ms (not 50 * 50ms = 2500ms for serial execution)
test_cases.expected_result: 50 rules processed in 10 batches of RULE_CONCURRENCY=5, completing in roughly 10x the single-rule latency instead of 50x, demonstrating effective concurrent throughput. The take:50 limit in findMany bounds the maximum rules per invocation.

test_cases.title: AutomationRule deletion cascades AutomationLog entries
steps: Create AutomationRule with id=R
Create AutomationLog entries with automationRuleId=R
Verify logs exist: prisma.automationLog.count({ where: { automationRuleId: R } }) > 0
Delete AutomationRule via prisma.automationRule.delete({ where: { id: R } })
Query prisma.automationLog.findFirst({ where: { automationRuleId: R } })
test_cases.expected_result: After AutomationRule deletion, all linked AutomationLog entries are cascade-deleted because AutomationLog defines: automationRule AutomationRule @relation(fields: [automationRuleId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)

test_cases.title: createPerMeetingAutomation blocks adding automations to CANCELLED/COMPLETED meetings
steps: Create meeting with status CANCELLED
Call createPerMeetingAutomation({ meetingId, triggerType: 'MEETING_REMINDER', actionType: 'SEND_NOTIFICATION', actionConfig: {} })
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'לא ניתן להוסיף אוטומציה לפגישה שהושלמה או בוטלה' }
Repeat with meeting status COMPLETED, verify same error
test_cases.expected_result: createPerMeetingAutomation checks meeting.status and returns error 'לא ניתן להוסיף אוטומציה לפגישה שהושלמה או בוטלה' for CANCELLED or COMPLETED meetings, preventing users from adding automations to terminal-state meetings

test_cases.title: SEND_NOTIFICATION auto-sets recipientId to current user when not provided
steps: Authenticate as user with id=42
Call createGlobalMeetingAutomation({ triggerType: 'MEETING_BOOKED', actionType: 'SEND_NOTIFICATION', actionConfig: { message: 'New booking!' } })
Note: actionConfig.recipientId is not provided
Verify the code branch: if (actionType === 'SEND_NOTIFICATION' && !finalActionConfig?.recipientId) sets recipientId to currentUser.id
Verify the created rule's actionConfig includes { message: 'New booking!', recipientId: 42 }
test_cases.expected_result: When actionType is SEND_NOTIFICATION and recipientId is not in actionConfig, the system auto-fills recipientId with currentUser.id (42), ensuring the notification recipient is always defined

test_cases.title: Per-meeting trigger types restricted to MEETING_REMINDER and MEETING_CANCELLED only
steps: Call createPerMeetingAutomation({ meetingId: 'mtg1', triggerType: 'MEETING_BOOKED', actionType: 'SEND_NOTIFICATION', actionConfig: {} })
Verify PER_MEETING_TRIGGERS = new Set(['MEETING_REMINDER', 'MEETING_CANCELLED'])
Verify 'MEETING_BOOKED' is not in PER_MEETING_TRIGGERS
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'סוג טריגר לא תקין לאוטומציה לפגישה' }
test_cases.expected_result: createPerMeetingAutomation rejects MEETING_BOOKED as a trigger type because only MEETING_REMINDER and MEETING_CANCELLED are in the PER_MEETING_TRIGGERS set; returns error 'סוג טריגר לא תקין לאוטומציה לפגישה'

test_cases.title: Invalid triggerType rejected by validateAutomationInput
steps: Call createGlobalMeetingAutomation({ triggerType: 'INVALID_TRIGGER', actionType: 'WEBHOOK', actionConfig: {} })
Verify VALID_TRIGGER_TYPES = Set(['MEETING_BOOKED', 'MEETING_CANCELLED', 'MEETING_REMINDER'])
Verify 'INVALID_TRIGGER' is not in the set
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'סוג טריגר לא תקין' }
test_cases.expected_result: validateAutomationInput returns 'סוג טריגר לא תקין' for any triggerType not in VALID_TRIGGER_TYPES set

test_cases.title: Invalid actionType rejected by validateAutomationInput
steps: Call createGlobalMeetingAutomation({ triggerType: 'MEETING_BOOKED', actionType: 'INVALID_ACTION', actionConfig: {} })
Verify VALID_ACTION_TYPES = Set(['SEND_NOTIFICATION', 'CALCULATE_DURATION', 'UPDATE_RECORD_FIELD', 'SEND_WHATSAPP', 'WEBHOOK', 'CREATE_TASK'])
Verify 'INVALID_ACTION' is not in the set
Verify response is { success: false, error: 'סוג פעולה לא תקין' }
test_cases.expected_result: validateAutomationInput returns 'סוג פעולה לא תקין' for any actionType not in VALID_ACTION_TYPES set

test_cases.title: createFolder creates root-level folder with companyId filter

test_cases.expected_result: Folder 'Documents' created with companyId matching user's company; appears in getStorageData(null) response with serialized dates (toISOString()) and _count.files field

test_cases.expected_result: Child folder 'Q1-Reports' created with parentId referencing 'Projects'; appears in getStorageData when navigating to parent folder

test_cases.title: saveFileMetadata persists file and returns downloadUrl

Verify no duplicate found (prisma.file.findFirst by key returns null)
Verify returned object includes { id, name: 'report.pdf', size: 5242880, type: 'application/pdf', downloadUrl: '/api/files/{id}/download' }
test_cases.expected_result: saveFileMetadata returns the new file with downloadUrl matching pattern /api/files/{id}/download; file persisted with companyId, folderId=null, recordId=null

test_cases.title: moveFileToFolder moves file and calls revalidatePath('/files')
steps: Create folder 'Archive' at root level -> archiveId
Upload file at root (folderId=null) -> fileId

Verify prisma.folder.findFirst verifies folder belongs to company

Verify revalidatePath('/files') is called
Call getStorageData(archiveId) and verify file appears
test_cases.expected_result: File's folderId updated to Archive folder's id; file appears in getStorageData(archiveId) after revalidatePath('/files')

test_cases.title: renameFolder updates name via updateMany with companyId filter

test_cases.title: deleteFile removes from database and calls UTApi.deleteFiles with key
steps: Upload file with key='ut-key-xyz' -> fileId
Call deleteFile(fileId)
Verify prisma.file.findFirst({ where: { id, companyId } }) returns the file with key
Verify prisma.file.delete({ where: { id, companyId } }) is called
Verify UTApi.deleteFiles('ut-key-xyz') is called for external cleanup
Verify revalidatePath('/files') is called
